-  MOS 0322, Reconnaissance Marine, Sniper Qualified (MGySgt to Pvt) NMOS
## (0321), DC PP&O
a.  Summary.  The Reconnaissance Sniper conducts precision fires
utilizing light, medium, and heavy scoped rifles.  Employed as part of the
Marine Air-Ground Task Force, joint task force or joint force, the
Reconnaissance Sniper is an expert in low visibility, multi-domain collection
operations, and provides the reconnaissance team with a capability to
precisely shape the operational environment through the delivery of precision
direct fire.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Have no history of mental illness.
(2) Must have a current first class PFT.
(3) Must hold MOS 0321, Reconnaissance Marine.
(4) Must have a minimum GT Score of 105 or higher.
(5) Must be currently qualified as an expert rifleman.
(6) Security requirement:  secret security clearance required.
c.  Requirements.  Reconnaissance Marines must successfully complete the
Reconnaissance Sniper Course (M100Z32).  Additionally, Reconnaissance Marines
(PMOS 0321) who have previously completed Sniper training via Scout Sniper
Basic Course at WTBN Quantico (M0281Z9), SOI-E (M0381Z4), SOI-W (M1081Z2) may
submit documentation of Scout Sniper Basic Course completion to Deputy
Commandant, Plans, Policies, & Operations (POF) for awarding of MOS 0322.

## 3110 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-29 Enclosure (1)
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ca), the Reconnaissance Training and Readiness Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## Special Forces 55-3018.
f.  Related Military Skill.  Reconnaissance Marine, 0321.
-  MOS 0323, Reconnaissance Marine, Parachute Qualified (MGySgt to Pvt) NMOS
## (0321), DC PP&O
a.  Summary.  Reconnaissance Marine, Parachute Qualified, conduct low
level static line (LLSL) or military freefall (MFF) airborne operations to
clandestinely gain access to the operating environment to conduct full-
spectrum amphibious reconnaissance, ground reconnaissance, preparation of the
operational environment and shaping, and specialized raid missions in support
of the division, MEF, other MAGTFs, or joint force.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must hold MOS 0321, Reconnaissance Marine.
(2) Maintain physical fitness specified by COMNAVMEDCOM standards.
(3) Must have current Physically Qualified (PQ) Naval Special
Warfare/Special Operations Physical to perform HALO/HAHO duties in accordance
with inter-service requirements established in reference (ai) and standards
of medical fitness.
c.  Requirements
(1) Must complete the Basic Airborne Course (A030CG1).
(2) Must complete the Multi Mission Parachute Course (M50KLD1).
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ca), Reconnaissance Training and Readiness Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## Special Forces 55-3018.
f.  Related Military Skill
## (1) Rifleman, 0311.
## (2) Reconnaissance Marine, 0321.
## (3) Reconnaissance Marine, Sniper Qualified, 0322.
## (4) Reconnaissance Marine, Combatant Diver Qualified, 0324.
(5) Reconnaissance Marine, Parachute and Combatant Diver Qualified,
## 0326.
## (6) Marine Raider, 0372.


## 3110 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-30 Enclosure (1)
-  MOS 0324, Reconnaissance Marine, Combatant Diver Qualified (MGySgt to
Pvt) NMOS (0321), DC PP&O
a.  Summary.  Reconnaissance Marine, Combatant Diver Qualified, conduct
underwater operations to facilitate amphibious reconnaissance, underwater
reconnaissance, ground reconnaissance, surveillance, preparation of the
operational environment and shaping, and specialized raid missions in support
of the division, MEF, other MAGTFs, or joint force.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must hold MOS 0321, Reconnaissance Marine.
(2) Must have passed the USSOCOM in test and the physical screening
test in accordance with USSOCOM Manual 350-4V1 and MILPERSMAN 1220-410 (Seal/
EOD/SWCC/diver/air physical screening testing standards and procedures)
within 30 days before course attendance.
(3) Must have current Physically Qualified (PQ) Naval Special
Warfare/Special Operations Physical to perform combatant diver duties in
accordance with inter-service requirements established in reference (ai) and
standards of medical fitness.
c.  Requirements
(1) Must complete the Combatant Diver Qualification Course (N20L6H1).
(2) Maintain physical fitness specified by COMNAVMEDCOM standards.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer
to reference (ca), Reconnaissance Training and Readiness Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## Special Forces 55-3018.
f.  Related Military Skill
## (1) Rifleman, 0311.
## (2) Reconnaissance Marine, Sniper Qualified, 0322.
## (3) Reconnaissance Marine, 0321.
## (4) Reconnaissance Marine, Parachute Qualified, 0323.
(5) Reconnaissance Marine, Parachute and Combatant Diver Qualified,
## 0326.
## (6) Marine Raider, 0372.
## (7) Combat Rubber Reconnaissance Craft Coxswain Skills Course, 8020.
-  MOS 0326, Reconnaissance Marine, Parachute and Combatant Diver Qualified
(MGySgt to Pvt) NMOS (0321), DC PP&O
a.  Summary.  Reconnaissance Marine, Parachute and Combatant Diver
Qualified, conduct airborne and subsurface operations to clandestinely gain
access to the operating environment to conduct full-spectrum amphibious

## 3110 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-31 Enclosure (1)
reconnaissance, ground reconnaissance & surveillance, preparation of the
operational environment and shaping, and specialized raid missions in support
of the division, MEF, other MAGTFs, or joint force.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must hold MOS 0321, Reconnaissance Marine.
(2) Must have current Physically Qualified (PQ) Naval Special
Warfare/Special Operations Physical to perform combatant diver and HALO/ HAHO
duties in accordance with inter-service requirements established in reference
(ai) and Standards of Medical Fitness.
(3) Must have passed the USSOCOM in test and the physical screening
test in accordance with USSOCOM Manual 350-4V1 and MILPERSMAN 1220-410 (Seal/
EOD/SWCC/Diver/Air Physical Screening Testing Standards and Procedures)
within 30 days before Combatant Diver Course (N20L6H1) attendance.
c.  Requirements
(1) Must complete the Basic Airborne Course (A030CG1).
(2) Must complete the Multi Mission Parachute Course (M50KLD1).
(3) Must complete the Combatant Diver Qualification Course (N20L6H1).
(4) Maintain physical fitness standards specified by COMNAVMEDCOM.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ca), the Reconnaissance Training and Readiness Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## Special Forces 55-3018.
f.  Related Military Skill
## (1) Rifleman, 0311.
## (2) Reconnaissance Marine, 0321.
## (3) Reconnaissance Marine, Sniper Qualified, 0322.
## (4) Reconnaissance Marine, Parachute Qualified, 0323.
## (5) Reconnaissance Marine, Combatant Diver Qualified, 0324.
## (6) Marine Raider, 0372.
## (7) Combat Rubber Reconnaissance Craft Coxswain, 8020.
-  MOS 0327, Reconnaissance Team Leader (SSgt to Sgt) NMOS (0321), DC PP&O
a.  Summary.  The Reconnaissance Team Leader is a reconnaissance Marine
with the training and experience to lead Marines in combat swimming, small
boat operations, highly refined scouting and long-range patrolling, close
combat skills, demolitions, forward observation for supporting arms, initial
terminal guidance operations for heliborne, airborne, and waterborne forces,
long-range communications, multi-domain collections, threat weapons and

## 3110 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-32 Enclosure (1)
equipment identification.  The Reconnaissance Team Leader is responsible for
ensuring that their recon team is trained, prepared, and equipped for multi-
domain reconnaissance and armed combat as it relates to assigned missions.
This includes, but is not limited to, mental and physical preparation, the
tactics, techniques, procedures required to accomplish the assigned mission,
and the supervision of the establishment and operation of unit command and
control to include the integration and synchronization of the warfighting
functions.  Recon Team Leaders plan, brief, and execute a broad range of
reconnaissance missions in offsite, austere, uncertain, and contested
environments with MAGTF and joint integration across the competition
continuum.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must be a volunteer.
(2) Must pass the Marine Corps Water Survival Advanced (WSA) Swim
## Qualification.
(3) Must pass the USMC physical fitness test (PFT) with a 1st class
score of 235 or higher.
(4) Must complete a five-hundred yard fin in a time limit of fifteen
minutes.  The fin must be conducted as an individual with a fighting load,
pack, and T/O weapon.
(5) Must be medically qualified to perform combatant diver and HALO/
HAHO duties.
(6) Security requirement:  must have secret security clearance.
c.  Requirements.  Marines must complete Reconnaissance Leader Course
(M10NAN2) for awarding of the 0327 MOS.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ca), Reconnaissance Training and Readiness Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## None.
f.  Related Military Skill
## (1) Reconnaissance Marine, 0321.
## (2) Reconnaissance Marine, Sniper Qualified, 0322.
## (3) Reconnaissance Marine, Parachute Qualified, 0323.
## (4) Reconnaissance Marine, Combatant Diver Qualified, 0324.
(5) Reconnaissance Marine, Parachute and Combatant Diver Qualified,
## 0326.
-  MOS 0331, Machine Gunner (Sgt to Pvt) PMOS, DC PP&O
a.  Summary.  The Machine Gunner is responsible for the tactical
employment of the light, medium, and heavy machine guns, and their support
vehicle.  Machine Gunners provide direct fire in support of the infantry

## 3110 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-33 Enclosure (1)
rifle squads/platoons/companies.  They are located in the weapons platoons of
the rifle company and weapons company in the infantry battalion.
Noncommissioned officers are assigned as team leaders and squad leaders/
section leaders.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have a WS-B water survival.
(2) Must possess an EL score of 90 or GT score of 90 or MM score of
90 or higher.
(3) Must meet MOS classification standards as listed in the glossary
(pg. xiv) prior to graduating from recruit training.
(4) Must meet initial strength test standards associated with PEF
assignments as listed in the glossary (pg.  xiv) prior to shipping to recruit
training.
c.  Requirements
(1) Complete the Infantry Marine Course (M03RG34) SOI Camp Lejeune,
NC or (M10RG32) SOI Camp Pendleton, CA.
(2) Complete the Machine Gunner Course (M100332) SOI, Camp Pendleton,
CA or (M030334) SOI, Camp Lejeune, NC.
(3) Team/Squad leaders will complete the Advanced Machinegun Course
at (M10H6A2) SOI, Camp Pendleton, CA or (M03H6A4) SOI, Camp Lejeune, NC.
(4) Squad/Section leaders will complete the Infantry Small Unit
Leaders Course (M100302) SOI, Camp Pendleton, CA; or (M030304) SOI Camp
Lejeune, NC.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (e), Infantry Training and Readiness Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## Infantry 55-3016.00.
f.  Related Military Skill.  Rifleman, 0311.
-  MOS 0341, Mortarman (Sgt to Pvt) PMOS, DC PP&O
a.  Summary.  Mortarmen are responsible for the tactical employment of
the light and medium mortar systems.  Mortarmen provide indirect fire in
support to the rifle and LAR squads/platoons/companies and also the infantry
and LAR battalions.  They are located in the weapons platoon of the rifle
companies, 81mm mortar platoons of the weapons companies, and LAR companies.
Noncommissioned officers are assigned as mortar gunners, forward observers,
fire direction plotters, and squad and section leaders.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have a WS-B water survival.
(2) Must possess a CL score of 90 or GT score of 90 or higher.

## 3110 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-34 Enclosure (1)
(3) Must meet MOS classification standards as listed in the glossary
(pg. xiv) prior to graduating from recruit training.
(4) Must meet initial strength test standards associated with PEF
assignments as listed in the glossary (pg. xiv) prior to shipping to recruit
training.
c.  Requirements
(1) Complete the Infantry Marine Course (M03RG34) SOI Camp Lejeune,
NC or (M10RG32) SOI Camp Pendleton, CA.
(2) Complete the Infantry Mortarman Course (M030344) SOI, Camp
Lejeune, NC or (M100342) SOI Camp Pendleton, CA.
(3) Team/squad leaders will complete the Advanced Mortarman Course
(M10H5H2) at SOI Camp Pendleton, CA or (M03H5H4) at SOI, Camp Lejeune, NC.
(4) Squad/section leaders will complete the Infantry Small Unit
Leaders Course (M100302) at SOI Camp Pendleton, CA or (M030304) at SOI Camp
Lejeune, NC.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (e), Infantry Training and Readiness Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## Infantry 55-3016.
f.  Related Military Skill
## (1) Rifleman, 0311.
## (2) Field Artillery Cannoneer, 0811.
-  MOS 0352, Antitank Missile Gunner (Sgt to Pvt) PMOS, DC PP&O
a.  Summary.  The Antitank Missile Gunner is responsible for the tactical
employment of the M41A7 Improved Target Acquisition System (ITAS) Saber and
M98A1 Javelin Weapons System during anti-armor operations and tactical
vehicle operations.  They provide medium and heavy anti-armor fire in support
of the infantry battalion, LAR battalion, and the MAGTF.  They are located in
the anti-armor platoon within the weapons company of the infantry battalions
and LAR battalions.  Noncommissioned officers are assigned as gunners and
squad leaders.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have a WS-B water survival.
(2) Must possess a GT score of 95 or MM score of 95 or higher.
(3) Must have normal color vision, and vision of 20/200, correctable
to 20/20.
(4) Must meet MOS classification standards as listed in the glossary
(pg. xiv) prior to graduating from recruit training.

## 3110 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-35 Enclosure (1)
(5) Must have a past driving record appropriate for issue of SF-46 as
evidenced by a search of the National Driver Register.
(6) Must meet initial strength test standards associated with PEF
assignments as listed in the glossary (pg. xiv) prior to shipping to recruit
training.
(7) Must possess the psychological and physiological qualifications
required for licensing as a government motor vehicle operator (see reference
(ar) and (as)).
c.  Requirements
(1) Complete the Infantry Marine Course (M03RG34) SOI Camp Lejeune,
NC or (M10RG32) SOI Camp Pendleton, CA.
(2) Complete the Infantry Anti-Tank Missile Gunner Course (M10T2B2)
at SOI Camp Pendleton, CA or (M03T2B4) at SOI, Camp Lejeune, NC.
(3) Team/Squad leaders will complete the Advanced Anti-Tank Missile
Gunner Course (M10MUR2) at SOI Camp Pendleton, CA or (M03MUR4) at SOI Camp
Lejeune, NC.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (e), Infantry Training and Readiness Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Artillery and Missile Crew Members 55-3014.00.
f.  Related Military Skill.  Rifleman, 0311.
-  MOS 0363, Light Armored Reconnaissance Unit Leader (GySgt to SSgt) PMOS,
## DC PP&O #
a.  Summary.  The Light Armored Reconnaissance (LAR) Unit Leader serves
as the enlisted technical and tactical advisor to the LAR unit commander.
He/She assists the commander with all aspects of the planning, training,
employment, logistical support, maintenance, accountability, welfare, and
administrative functions in support of the LAR unit’s mission.  He/She
controls the employment of his vehicle and section within the unit and
assists with the coordination and implementation of supporting arms.  He/She
supervises and coordinates the performance of maintenance, maintenance
management, embarkation, and recovery operations.  Staff Sergeants are
employed as LAR platoon sergeants and gunnery sergeants are employed as LAR
company operation chiefs.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have WS-I water survival qualification.
(2) Security requirement:  must be TS/SCI security eligible.
(3) Must have held PMOS 0313, LAR Marine.  SSgts with less than 2
years TIG are eligible to LATMOV into PMOS 0313 and will be retained/promoted
in accordance with career progression for OccFld.


## 3110 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-36 Enclosure (1)
c.  Requirements
(1) Must complete the Light Armored Reconnaissance Vehicle Commanders
Course (M10H3V2) prior to attending Light Armored Reconnaissance Leader
## Course.
(2) Must complete the Light Armored Reconnaissance Leader Course
(M10H3W2) prior to being assigned the PMOS 0363.
(3) GySgt’s must complete the Ground Combat Element (GCE) Operations
Specialist Course (N03KBC1/N30RCC1).
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (de), Light Armored Reconnaissance (LAR) Training and Readiness
(T&R) Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code
(1) First-Line Supervisor of Weapons Specialist/Crew Members 55-2012.
## (2) Infantry 55-3016.
## (3) Armored Assault Vehicle Crew Members 55-3013.
f.  Related Military Skill
## (1) Light Armored Reconnaissance Marine, 0313.
## (2) Reconnaissance Marine, 0321.
## (3) Infantry Unit Leader, 0369.
## (4) Ground Operations Specialist, 8711.
# When promoted to SSgt, the 0363 MOS will only be awarded after completing
the MOS requirements.  To facilitate M&RA with assignments, Marines may be
assigned using the Intended MOS for which they were selected until they have
met the requirements.
-  MOS 0365, Infantry Squad Leader (Sgt) NMOS (0311, 0331, 0341, 0352), DC
## PP&O
a.  Summary.  THIS IS AN OBSOLETE MOS, NO LONGER IN USE IN THE TABLE OF
## ORGANIZATION OF THE MARINE CORPS.  THIS MOS IS MAINTAINED IN THE MOS MANUAL
## BECAUSE IT APPEARS IN THE RECORDS OF MARINES STILL ON ACTIVE DUTY OR THE
RESERVES.  Infantry Squad Leaders are created via the squad leader
development program (SLDP) by a lateral move from the infantry MOS of
Rifleman (0311), Machine Gunner (0331), Mortarman (0341), or Antitank Missile
Gunner (0352).  SLDP was designed to synchronize the training and education
of a second term infantryman with the assignments process that results in a
more mature, better trained and educated squad leader.  Volunteers gain
priority placement in advanced infantry training and the Sergeants Course
which professionalizes the squad leader billet and enhances the capabilities
of Marine infantry squad leaders for operations in complex and distributed
environments.  The Infantry Squad Leader plans for assigned missions and
carries out the orders issued by the platoon commander.  He/She is also
responsible for the discipline, training, control, conduct, and welfare of
his squad at all times, as well as the condition, care, and economical and

## 3110 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-37 Enclosure (1)
effective use of its weapons and equipment.  In combat, he is responsible for
the tactical employment, fire discipline, fire control, sustainment, and
maneuver of his squad during offensive, defensive, amphibious, and stability
operations.  These leaders will be placed in squad billets based off original
MOS proficiency and strength.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) No courts martial convictions.
(2) No adverse fitness reports within the last 12 months.
(3) Must be a volunteer in the grade of corporal or sergeant.
(4) No derogatory Page 11 entries within the last 12 months.
(5) Must have a minimum of 3 and no more than 6 years in service.
(6) Must be complete in all professional military education
requirements for corporal.
(7) No non-judicial punishments (NJPs) within the last 12 months and
with no more than 2 NJPs during the current contract.
(8) Must be a graduate of:
(a) Advanced Mortar Course (M10H5H2 or M03H5H4).
(b) Advanced Machinegun Course (M10H6A2 or M03H6A4).
(c) Advanced Anti-Armor Course (M10MUR2 or M03MUR4).
(d) Scout Sniper Course (M0281Z9 or M0381Z4 or M1081Z2).
(e) Advanced Infantry Marine Course (M10M3X2 or M03M3X4).
c.  Requirements
(1) Complete the Sergeants Course at one of the following locations:
(a) (M02T4M5), Staff Noncommissioned Officers Academy, Quantico,
## VA.
(b) (M09T4M2), Staff Noncommissioned Officers Academy, 29 Palms,
## CA.
(c) (M21T4M1), Staff Noncommissioned Officers Academy, Kaneohe
Bay, HI.
(d) (M03T4M5), Staff Noncommissioned Officers Academy, Camp,
Lejeune, NC.
(e) (M22T4M1), Staff Noncommissioned Officers Academy, FPO AP
## Okinawa, Japan.
(f) (M10T4M6), Staff Noncommissioned Officers Academy, MCB Camp
Pendleton, CA.

## 3110 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-38 Enclosure (1)
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (e), Infantry Training and Readiness Manual and Marine Corps
Warfighting Publication 11.2-Marine Rifle Squad.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## None.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 0367, Light Armored Reconnaissance Master Gunner (MGySgt to Sgt)
## NMOS (0313, 0363, 0393) DC PP&O
a.  Summary.  The Light Armored Reconnaissance (LAR) Master Gunner is a
MGySgt to Sgt specifically trained in the training and employment of LAR
company and battalion organic weapons, gear and assigned personnel and in the
basics of LAR Gunnery as defined by the current version of MCRP 3-10D.1 Light
Armored Vehicle-25 Gunnery and Employment.  The Battalion Master Gunners and
Company Master Gunners are special staff officers employed as the principal
advisor to their respective commander.  They assist in the development of
training and employment plans designed to ensure mission essential task
compliance.  They help design and vet the gunnery and training policies of
the commander and help to disseminate information to the unit’s personnel
regarding such policies.  They generate and quantify reports on the unit’s
technical and tactical gunnery proficiency and brief the unit commander as to
where each subordinate unit sits in regard to his intent.  They advise/mentor
the officers and Marines of the unit in all applicable mechanical, doctrinal
and conceptual gunnery and training matters as required.  Ultimately, they
will provide improvements to the general efficiency, effectiveness and
proficiency of the command.  They have oversight of the unit's ammunition
allocation and semi-annual gunnery qualification and certification programs.
Additional duties will include participation in applicable Course Curriculum
Review Boards (CCRB), new curriculum development for gunnery and LAR related
tasks and vetting within the LAR Training and Readiness Manual (T&R).
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have a GT score of 100 or above.
(2) Must have an ECL score of 90 or above.
(3) Must have held the PMOS of 0313 or 0363.
(4) The student must be a Sgt (E-5) through GySgt (E-7) or equivalent
rank.
(5) Qualified within the last 12 months on crew qualification table
six.
(6) Certified as a Light Armored Vehicle Crew Evaluator within the
last 12 months.
(7) The student must have one year of obligated service remaining
upon completion of the course.
(8) Must have successfully completed the Light Armored Reconnaissance
Commander Course (M10H3V2).

## 3110 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-39 Enclosure (1)
c.  Requirements.  Must complete the Light Armored Reconnaissance Master
Gunner Course (M10H212) Camp Pendleton, CA.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (dc), Light Armored Reconnaissance Training and Readiness Manual
## (T&R).
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## Tactical Ground Range Control Officer.
f.  Related Military Skill.  Assault Amphibious Master Gunner, 1868.
-  MOS 0369, Infantry Unit Leader (GySgt to SSgt) PMOS, DC PP&O
a.  Summary.  The Infantry Unit Leader is responsible to the commander
and assists in the planning, training, deployment and employment of all
infantry organic weapons systems and the units combat power capabilities.
They supervise and coordinate the preparation and assignment of personnel/
equipment in accordance with the table of organization/table of equipment of
their unit.  Supervises the establishment and operation of unit command and
control to include the integration and synchronization of the warfighting
functions.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Attain promotion to SSgt.
(2) Have held one of the following 0311, 0331, 0341, or 0352.
(3) Security requirement:  secret security clearance required.
c.  Requirements
(1) Complete the Infantry Unit Leader Course (M03H5K4) at School of
Infantry, Camp Lejeune, NC or (M10H5K2) at School of Infantry (West), Camp
Pendleton, CA (reserve component is waived from this requirement).
(2) Marines eligible for promotion consideration to the rank of
gunnery sergeant that do not have the 0369 PMOS will not be eligible for
promotion consideration and will be deleted from the promotion board
population.
(3) Marines with 24 or more months’ time in grade as a staff sergeant
that have not completed the Infantry Unit Leader Course will require a CG
endorsement for further retention.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (e), Infantry Training and Readiness Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
First-Line Supervisors/Managers of Weapons Specialists/Crew Members 55-2012.
f.  Related Military Skill
(1) Operations and Tactics Instructor, 0577.
(2) Ground Command and Control (C2) Operations NCO, 8711.

## 3110 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-40 Enclosure (1)
(3) Maneuver Warfare Specialist (MWS), 8713.
-  MOS 0372, Marine Raider (MGySgt to Sgt) PMOS, DC PP&O #
a.  Summary.  The Marine Raider is a US Marine who is trained to execute
missions within the following special operations core activities assigned by
USSOCOM: direct action (DA), special reconnaissance (SR), countering weapons
of mass destruction (CWMD), counterterrorism (CT), unconventional warfare
(UW), foreign internal defense (FID), security force assistance (SFA), and
counterinsurgency (COIN). The Marine Raider is capable of being independently
employed or rapidly integrated within the joint force. As part of a command
team within the O4 level and above, the MGySgt or MSgt Marine Raider is in
designated billets as the Special Operations Forces Senior Enlisted Advisor
(SOFSEA). The MGySgt Marine Raider can also serve as a Command Senior
Enlisted Leader (CSEL) in designated combined, joint, and SOF billets
(examples combined joint special operations task force (CJSOTF), special
operations task force (SOTF), and theater special operations command (TSOC).
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must be a U.S. citizen.
(2) Must be world-wide deployable.
(3) Must have a current first class PFT.
(4) Must have a GT score of 105 or higher.
(5) Must meet the minimum Ground Combat Arms MOS Classification
## Standards.
(6) Must obtain a Naval Special Warfare/Special Operator (NSW/SO)
physical prior to attending A&S.
(7) Any administrative action, adverse in nature, or jeopardy on
contract will require a COMMARFORSOC waiver to attend A&S and ITC.
(8) FTAP (LCpl to Sgt), any MOS with a minimum of 24 months TIS may
volunteer to attend MARFORSOC assessment and selection (A&S).  STAP (LCpl to
Sgt), any MOS may attend A&S with the approval from their respective MOS
## Monitor.
(9) Must be at least a Corporal, with no more than eight years total
time in service upon attending the MARFORSOC Individual Training Course (ITC)
(M03KZ4K).  Sergeant(s) must report to ITC with no more than 18 months’ time
in grade (TIG).  COMMARFORSOC may waive TIG requirements for Marines
considered exceptionally qualified based on the organization's operational
requirements.
(10) Must meet the following physical standards during phase 1 of
## A&S:
(a) First class PFT.
(b) First class CFT.
(c) Not exceeding a 40:00 maximum time for a 5-mile run.

## 3110 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-41 Enclosure (1)
(d) Not exceeding an 8:00 maximum time while conducting two
repetitions of the obstacle course.
(e) Water survival intermediate (WSI), to include not exceeding a
13:00 maximum time for a 300-meter swim using the breaststroke or side
stroke.
(f) Not exceeding a 3:00:00 maximum time for a 12-mile ruck march
with a 45 lbs. ruck in MARPAT utilities and rifle (NOTE:  water, utilities,
and rifle weight not included).
(11) Security requirement:
(a) Completed finalized adjudicated secret clearance based on a
national agency check, law enforcement and credit check (NACLC) or T3
background investigation prior to attending ITC (NOTE:  interim secret
clearance eligibility will not be accepted).
(b) Meet TS/SCI eligibility guidelines upon ITC graduation and
assignment to the Marine raider regiment.
c.  Requirements
(1) Selection from MARFORSOC A&S to attend ITC.  Graduate of ITC
(M03KZ4K).  Successful completion of the Survival, Evasion, Resistance, and
Escape (SERE) Level C Course (M03M47K) or a joint equivalent SERE Level C
course.  An approval letter to lateral move to PMOS 0372 (NOTE:  COMMARFORSOC
has been delegated awarding authority for the PMOS 0372).
(2) Once awarded the PMOS 0372, the Marine Raider is authorized to
wear the Marine Raider Insignia (MRI) (NOTE: In accordance MCO 1020.35
involuntary removal of the MRI, COMMARFORSOC may revoke this privilege from a
## Marine Raider).
(3) Once awarded the PMOS 0372, the Marine Raider must have all
required credentials for rapid global deployment in accordance with the DoD
## Foreign Clearance Guide.
(4) Required follow on training for all Marine Raiders prior to
assignment to the Marine Raider Regiment are as follows: Basic Language
Course (M03RRSK), Basic Airborne Course (A030CG1), and the Multi-Mission
Parachutist Course (M50KLD1).
(5) Marine Raiders are required to maintain a current first class PFT
and CFT to retain their PMOS as 0372. COMMARFORSOC can waiver this
requirement on a case by case basis.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (bw) NAVMAC 3500.97B, Marine Raider Regiment Training and Readiness
## Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## Special Forces 55-3018.
f.  Related Military Skill
(1) Counterintelligence/Human Intelligence Specialist, 0211.

# MOS 0372 Section Note: Serve in a joint special operations, MAGTF, SOCOM,
or theater special operations command (TSOC) billet
-  MOS 0393, Light Armored Reconnaissance Operations Chief (MGySgt to MSgt)
## PMOS, DC PP&O #
a.  Summary.  The Light Armored Reconnaissance Operations Chief (LAR Ops
chief) serves as the senior enlisted tactical advisor to the commander and
staff of a light armored reconnaissance (LAR) company or battalion.  They
advise the commander in the tactical employment of the organic weapons
systems of the unit, the condition, care, and economical use of unit
resources and equipment.  They coordinate and supervise all aspects of the
company or battalion combat operations center (COC) or administrative
logistics operations center (ALOC).  They ensure that personnel are trained
and proficient in the employment of C4I systems and manages the flow of
information provided through them.  They assist with the timely collection,
review, and distribution of orders, messages, briefs, reports, training
schedules and all tasks in support of the units core and assigned mission
essential tasks.  The LAR Operations Chief advises and makes recommendations
for the planning, coordination, and execution of the units fire support plan.
They manage the 03XX personnel within the unit, in order to minimize crew
turbulence amongst formed and trained crews, assists with the development of
the units training plan, and managing the unit readiness program.  They
coordinate and supervise the embarkation and debarkation of the unit.  Master
Sergeants are assigned as battalion logistics chiefs, assistant battalion
operations chiefs, and headquarters & service company operations chiefs.

## 3110 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-43 Enclosure (1)
Master Gunnery Sergeants are assigned as A/C and R/C battalion operations
chiefs, Marine Corps warfighting lab (MCWL) operations chief, Marine air
ground task force-training center (MAGTF-TC) operations chief, program
manager-light armored vehicle (PM-LAV) operations advisor, and plans,
policies, and operations (PP&O) LAR advocate/OccFld sponsor.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have WS-I water survival qualification.
(2) Security requirement:  must be TS/SCI security eligible.
(3) Must have held the PMOS 0363, Light Armored Reconnaissance Unit
## Leader.
c.  Requirements
(1) Successful completion of the GCE Operations Specialist Course
(N30RCC1 or N30KBC1) prior to being assigned the PMOS 0393.
(2) 0393 MSgt’s and MGySgt’s must complete the Operations and Tactics
Instructor Chief Course (M09HE35).
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (de), Light Armored Reconnaissance (LAR) Training and Readiness
(T&R) Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code
(1) 55-1019 Military Officer Special and Tactical Operations Leaders.
(2) First-Line Supervisor of Weapons Specialist/Crew Members 55-2012.
## (3) Infantry 55-3016.
## (4) Armored Assault Vehicle Crew Members 55-3013.
f.  Related Military Skill
## (1) Light Armored Reconnaissance Marine, 0313.
## (2) Reconnaissance Marine, 0321.
## (3) Light Armored Reconnaissance Unit Leader, 0363.
## (4) Infantry Unit Leader, 0369.
(5) Infantry Operations chief, 0399.
(6) Operations and Tactics Instructor, 0577.
## (7) Ground Operations Specialist, 8711.
