-  MOS 5977, Weapons and Tactics Instructor Aviation Command & Control
(AC2) Maintenance Chief (MGySgt to GySgt) NMOS (5939, 5948, 5951, 5952, 5953,
## 5954, 5959, 5974, 5979, 5993), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Weapons and Tactics Instructor AC2 Maintenance Chiefs train
and evaluate Marine air command and control personnel on aviation sensors and
weapons systems in the employment of the Marine Air Command and Control
System (MACCS) to support the Marine Air Ground Task Force.
b.  Prerequisites.  Must hold MOS 5939, 5948, 5951, 5952, 5953, 5954,
5959, 5974, 5979, or 5993.
c.  Requirements.  Must successfully complete the MAWTS-1 Weapons and
Tactics Instructor Course (M149731).
d.  Duties
(1) Manage a unit's Weapons and Tactics Training Program.
(2) Perform classroom and operational instruction on various facets
of MACCS equipment/weapons systems including integration, employment
capabilities and limitations in support of the MAGTF and joint tasking.
(3) Analyze performance and provide corrective guidance.
(4) Instructs on current enemy capabilities and tactics to counter
their threat.
(5) Recommend the most qualified personnel for nomination to the
Weapons and Tactics Instructor Course and unit instructor qualifications.
(6) Ensures all training adheres to established training standards,
safety, and ORM procedures.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Teachers and Instructors, All Other 25-3099.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.

## 3137 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-350 Enclosure (1)
-  MOS 5979, Tactical Air Operations/Air Defense Systems Technician (GySgt
to Pvt) PMOS, DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Air Defense Systems Technicians are qualified to perform
and/or supervise the diagnosis, field-level repair, and modifications to
electronic systems and sub-systems found within selected units within the
Marine Air Control Group and the Marine Littoral Regiment.  Typical duties
include the installation, operation, troubleshooting, and maintenance of the
MADIS air defense RADAR systems (active and passive), radio communications
equipment, Tactical Data Link (TDL) equipment, networking and data storage
devices, and software/firmware configuration for Ground Based Air Defense
(GBAD) equipment.  These duties can be executed as part of a crew construct
as the System Configuration Manager or as part of a maintenance platoon.
Additional duties include the operation of TDLs, supervision of a maintenance
platoon, coordination of equipment evacuation for repair with other
maintenance activities, requisitioning of parts, and monitoring maintenance
reports.  These technicians also provide technical expertise in planning,
employing, installing, and integrating aviation command and control systems.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must be a U.S. citizen.
(2) Must have normal color vision.
(3) Must possess an EL score of 110 or higher.
(4) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.
c.  Requirements
(1) Complete the 5900 Commons Course (M091J31), Twentynine Palms, CA.
(2) Complete the Air Defense Systems Technician Course (M09KAS1),
Twentynine Palms, CA.
d.  Duties.  For a complete list of duties and tasks, refer to the Marine
Air Command and Control Systems (MACCS) Maintenance Training and Readiness
(T&R) Manual NAVMC 3500.128.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code
(1) Electrical and Electronics Repairers, Commercial and Industrial
## Equipment 49-2094.
## (2) Computer Operators 43-9011.
f.  Related Military Skill.  Ground Electronics Systems Maintenance
## Technician, 2862.
-  MOS 5993, Electronics Maintenance Chief (Aviation (C2)) (MGySgt to MSgt)
## PMOS, DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Electronics Maintenance Chiefs (Aviation (C2)) supervise,
coordinate, and instruct enlisted personnel in the operation and maintenance
of Marine Air Command and Control Systems (MACCS).  Provide information
concerning the employment, capabilities, limitations, and reliability of the
MACCS and other equipment.  Perform staff support duties in formulating and

## 3137 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-351 Enclosure (1)
implementing maintenance and supply plans, provisioning the MACCS, and
preparing publications and training materials.  Supervises surveying, siting,
installation, modification, and maintenance tasks.  Supervise Maintenance
Personnel in MOSs 5939, 5948, 5974, and 5979.  Must possess a detailed,
working knowledge of all applicable Marine Corps and Navy maintenance
programs.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must be a U.S. citizen.
(2) Must possess an EL score of 115 or higher.
(3) Must have previously held MOSs 5939, 5948, 5974, or 5979.
(4) Must possess knowledge of procedure directives and regulations
applicable to all functional areas of electronics maintenance.
(5) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.
Some operational billets may require top secret or top secret with sensitive
compartmented information eligibility.
c.  Requirements
(1) This MOS is career progression for MOSs 5939, 5948, 5974, and
## 5979.
(2) Successfully complete the MACCS Warrant Officer Course (M0968K1),
at Marine Corps Communications and Electronics School, Twentynine Palms, Ca
to receive the intended MOS (IMOS) of 5993I.
(3) After the completion of the MACCS Maintenance Course and upon
promotion to the rank of master sergeant, the MOS 5993 will be granted and
reported in MCTFS.
(4) Master Gunnery Sergeants in this MOS are highly encouraged to
attend the MATC Maintenance Managers Course (MMC) (N23KCN2) at NATTC
Pensacola, FL when appropriate space permits.
d.  Duties
(1) MGySgt and MSgt:
(a) Supports operational planning by providing commanders with
the capabilities, limitations, logistics and maintenance requirements of the
Marine Aviation Command and Control System equipment to include, but not
limited to, Aviation Command and Control Sensor Netting (AC2SN), Air Defense
RADAR, Communications, and Tactical Data Systems within the Marine Air
## Control Group.
(b) Coordinates maintenance activities of enlisted personnel
working on the MACCS.
(c) Inspects and schedules maintenance for MACCS maintenance
personnel.
(d) Instructs personnel in operation and maintenance of MACCS
equipment.

## 3137 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-352 Enclosure (1)
(e) Supervises and assists maintenance personnel in
requisitioning parts and supplies from the Marine Corps and Navy supply
system.
(f) Supervises surveying, siting, installation, or maintenance
tasks on MACCS.
(g) Ensures system/equipment performance evaluation and
qualification, problem identification, and to measure improvement efforts.
(h) Provides staff support in planning and implementing the
maintenance management of MACCS equipment.
(i) Assists the maintenance officer in the determination of
maintenance policy and the inventory management program of MACCS equipment.
(j) Identifying spectrum requirements and submitting spectrum
request for MACCS.
(2) MGySgt:
(a) Supervises and assists MACCS maintenance personnel in
requisitioning parts and supplies from the Marine Corps and Navy supply
system.
(b) Function as cognizant staff members in support of planning
for the employment and maintenance management of MACCS Equipment.
(c) Assists the maintenance officer in the determination of
maintenance policy and the inventory management program of MACCS equipment.
(d) Provide support in planning and determining spectrum
requirements and/or requests for MACCS equipment.

First-Line Supervisors/Managers of Mechanics, Installers, and Repairers 49-

## 3138 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-353 Enclosure (1)
## 3138.  OCCUPATIONAL FIELD 60, AIRCRAFT MAINTENANCE
-  Introduction.  The Aircraft Maintenance OccFlds includes direct and
indirect support of the total airframes and power plant package of all
aviation aircraft weapons systems.  While there is similarity in the skills
required to provide this support, the systems being supported are diverse..
The following examples are provided:  direct support-repair and servicing at
the organizational maintenance activity which is normally accomplished on
aircraft, e.g., preflight and servicing of aircraft and their systems;
indirect support-repair of components at the intermediate maintenance
activity which is normally accomplished off aircraft, e.g., hydraulic
actuators, servos, and accumulators, aircraft engines, and transmissions.
Marines entering these fields receive MOS 6000, Basic Aircraft Maintenance
Marine, and then progress through specific hard skill MOSs.  After completion
of formal training, Marines are qualified to maintain airframes and aircraft
components.  The opportunity to participate in a formal apprenticeship
program leading to receipt of a Department of Labor Certification of
Apprenticeship Completion maybe available in some MOSs within OccFlds 60/61/
-  As the Marine progresses, repair and administrative requirements for
multiple systems take on an equal importance until the Marine is placed in a
management/supervisory position.  Billets for both repair and administration
are varied and extend from the squadron level to staff positions at the wing,
force commander, and Headquarters U.S. Marine Corps level.
-  MOS 6011, Aviation Production Controller (MSgt to Sgt) NMOS (any PMOS
from 60/61/62/63/64/65), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Aviation Production Controllers (PC) are responsible for
planning, directing, and controlling scheduled and unscheduled maintenance
functions at the Intermediate Maintenance Activities (IMA) in direct support
of tiltrotor, rotary and fixed-wing aircraft, unmanned aerial systems, and
transient squadrons.  They supervise the IMA in repairing and requisitioning
of aeronautical components, consumables, tools and equipment, cryogenics,
ground support equipment, airborne armament equipment, and assist with
forecasting and managing the fiscal year logistical expenditures.  They must
be able to establish and effectively communicate goals across the
Maintenance, Avionics, and Ordnance Departments.  They are responsible with
ensuring the maintenance efforts are efficient, while maintaining constant
communication across all three departments, Supply and the Organizational
Maintenance Activities Maintenance Controllers.  Prepare or supervise the
preparation of reports, correspondence, schedules, and rosters pertaining to
maintenance and repair.  A high level of expertise is required to supervise
the execution of various tasks that are encompassed within a wide spectrum of
aviation maintenance and logistical matters.  They must possess a detailed,
working knowledge of Naval Aviation Logistics Command Management Information
Systems (NALCOMIS), Relational Supply (R-Supply) and all NAMP and processes
governed by reference (bs).  Strong written and verbal communication, and
computer skills are needed in the performance of their duties.
b.  Prerequisites.  Must possess a primary MOS from any of the following
OccFlds 60/61/62 Aircraft Maintenance, 63/64 Avionics, or 65 Ordnance.
c.  Requirements
(1) Must complete all 5000 level task lists in the Production Control
I-Level Training and Readiness Syllabus.  Shall be designated for a minimum

## 3138 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-354 Enclosure (1)
of 6 months and have attended the following courses in order to be awarded
the MOS:
(a) Intermediate Maintenance Activity (IMA) Production Control
Procedures Course (C-555-0043) or the Intermediate Maintenance Activity (IMA)
Power Plants Production Control (PC) Course (C-555-2021).
(b) Intermediate Maintenance Activity (IMA) Work Center
(Optimized) Course (C-555-0041).
d.  Duties.  For a listing of duties and tasks, refer to reference (bo),
Aviation Training and Readiness Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Production, Planning, and Expediting Clerks 43-5061.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 6012, Aviation Maintenance Controller (MSgt to Sgt) NMOS (any PMOS
from 60/61/62/63/64/65), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Aviation Maintenance Controllers are responsible for the
efficient attainment of aircraft and equipment readiness in support of
operational objectives.  Maintenance Control will manage all action to retain
or restore material or equipment to a serviceable condition with a minimum of
expenditure of resources.  They also supervise and manage the material
control center to ensure effective coordination with the Aviation Supply
Department (ASD) for all required indirect materials.  A high level of
management abilities and aviation maintenance experience is required to
supervise the execution of the various tasks encompassed within the wide
spectrum of aviation maintenance matters.  They must possess a detailed,
working knowledge of reference (ad) COMNAVAIRFORINST 4790.2. In addition,
Aviation Maintenance Controllers must have detailed knowledge of the
## Optimized Organizational Maintenance Activity Naval Aviation Logistics
Command Management Information Systems (OOMA/NALCOMIS).  They must also
possess a detailed, working knowledge of Naval Aviation Logistics Command
Management Information Systems (NALCOMIS), Relational Supply (R-Supply) and
all NAMP and processes governed by reference (bs).  Strong written and verbal
communication, and computer skills are needed in the performance of their
duties.
b.  Prerequisites.  Must possess a primary MOS from any of the following
OccFlds 60/61/62 Aircraft Maintenance, 63/64 Avionics, or 65 Aviation
## Ordnance.
c.  Requirements
(1) Must complete the following:
(a) Naval Aviation Maintenance Control Management for Optimized
Organizational Maintenance Activity (OOMA) (C-555-0053).
(b) All required “Safe-For-Flight” (SFF) training in accordance
with reference (ad) COMNAVFORINST 4790.2 and (bn) NAVMC 4790.1.
(c) Be SFF designated for a minimum of 6 months.


## 3138 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-355 Enclosure (1)
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ad) COMNAVAIRFORINST 4790.2 and (bn) NAVMC 4790.1.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code
(1) Production, Planning, and Expediting Clerks 43-5061.
(2) First-Line Supervisors of Mechanics, Installers, and Repairers
## 49-1011.
f.  Related Military Skill.  Aviation Production Control, 6011.
-  MOS 6016, Collateral Duty Inspector (MGySgt to LCpl) NMOS (any PMOS from
## 60/61/62/63/64/65), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Collateral Duty Inspectors (CDI) are experienced aviation
maintenance personnel assigned Quality Assurance (QA) duties, and are the
direct representative of the Commanding Officer for ensuring the quality of
aircraft, engines, components, and equipment.  CDIs are assigned to
production work centers where they supervise and inspect all work and comply
with the required QA inspections during all maintenance actions performed by
their respective work centers.  CDIs are not authorized to perform Quality
Assurance Representative level inspections.  CDIs are responsible to the QA
Officer when performing QA functions.  In addition to inspection duties, CDIs
serve as trainers and mentors in their areas of expertise.  CDIs must possess
the highest standards of professional integrity.
b.  Prerequisites.  Completion of the 4000 Level Training and Readiness
## Syllabus.
c.  Requirements.  Designation by the Commanding Officer or Maintenance
## Officer.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ad) COMNAVFORINST 4790.2 and (bn) NAVMC 4790.1.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code
(1) First-Line Supervisors of Mechanics, Installers, and Repairers
## 49-1011.
(2) Aircraft Mechanics and Service Technicians 49-3011.
(3) Cleaners of Vehicles and Equipment 53-7061.
f.  Related Military Skill
## (1) Collateral Duty Quality Assurance Representative, 6017.
## (2) Quality Assurance Representative, 6018.
-  MOS 6017, Collateral Duty Quality Assurance Representative (CDQAR) (GySgt
to Cpl) NMOS (any PMOS from 60/61/62/63/64/65), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  The Collateral Duty Quality Assurance Representative
(CDQAR) is a highly skilled person responsible for conducting and managing
the maintenance department’s QA effort.  CDQARs are assigned to production

## 3138 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-356 Enclosure (1)
work center but act in the same capacity as a Quality Assurance
Representative (QAR).
b.  Prerequisites.  None.
c.  Requirements.  Completion of the 5000 Level Training and Readiness
Syllabus.  Designation by the commanding officer or maintenance officer.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ad) COMNAVFORINST 4790.2 and (bn) NAVMC 4790.1.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code
(1) First-Line Supervisors of Mechanics, Installers, and Repairers
## 49-1011.
(2) Aircraft Mechanics and Service Technicians 49-3011.
(3) Cleaners of Vehicles and Equipment 53-7061.
f.  Related Military Skill
## (1) Collateral Duty Inspector, 6016.
## (2) Quality Assurance Representative, 6018.
-  MOS 6018, Quality Assurance Representative (QAR) (MSgt to Sgt) NMOS (any
PMOS from 60/61/62/63/64/65), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  The Quality Assurance Representative (QAR) is a highly
skilled person responsible for conducting and managing the maintenance
department’s quality assurance effort and are assigned to the quality
assurance work center (040).  These Aviation Professionals are skilled in
researching, reading, and interpreting drawings, maintenance technical
manuals, directives, and data.  They have the required skills to write with
clarity and technical accuracy and are committed to quality in all aspect of
naval aviation.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Completed the NALCOMIS (optimized) OMA Quality Assurance
Administration Course (C-555-0046).
(2) Completed the appropriate Aircraft Corrosion Course: OMA (N-701-
0013) or (C-6003183).  IMA MOS 6062 or 6092 QARs (N-701-0013) or (C-6003183).
c.  Requirements.  Complete the Quality Assurance Representative Training
and Readiness syllabus in addition to the completion of their MOS specific
5000 level Training and Readiness syllabus.  Designation by the Commanding
Officer or Maintenance Officer and served in the capacity of a QAR for a
minimum of 6 months.
d.  Duties.  For a listing of duties and tasks, refer to reference (bo),
Aviation Training and Readiness Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Production, Planning, and Expediting Clerks 43-5061.

## 3138 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-357 Enclosure (1)
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 6019, Aircraft Maintenance Chief (MGySgt to MSgt) PMOS, DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Aircraft Maintenance Chiefs are directly responsible to the
Commanding Officer for the establishment and sustainment of all maintenance
areas and functions within a fixed-wing, rotary-wing, tiltrotor, and aviation
logistics squadron or repair facility.  Their leadership and expertise are
essential to the success of the unit.  They are the Senior Enlisted
Maintenance Advisors to the Commanding Officer (CO) for all maintenance
Marines qualifications and professional development.  They report to the
Aircraft Maintenance Officer (AMO) on all matters affecting aircraft
maintenance and maintenance personnel.  They assist in the planning,
management, supervision, and execution of all scheduled and unscheduled
maintenance and flight operations.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Shall possess a primary MOS from any of the following OccFlds 60/
## 61/62, Aircraft Maintenance.
(2) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.
c.  Requirements.  See prerequisites.
d.  Duties.  For a complete list of duties refer to reference (ad)
COMNANAIRFORINST 4790.2 series and reference (bn) NAVMC 4790.1 series.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
First-Line Supervisors/Managers of Mechanics, Installers, and Repairers 49-
## 1011.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 6023, Aircraft Power Plants Test Cell Operator (GySgt to Cpl) NMOS
## (6122, 6123, 6124, 6216, 6222, 6223, 6227), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Aircraft Power Plants Test Cell Operators, inspect, test,
and perform corrective maintenance to aircraft gas turbine engines and engine
systems.  Perform test cell operations and minor repairs to test cells.  This
MOS will be assigned only to qualified Aircraft Power Plants Mechanics, MOSs
6122, 6123, 6124, 6222, 6223, 6226, and 6227.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Possess one of the following PMOSs 6122, 6123, 6124, 6216, 6222,
6223, 6226, and 6227.
(2) Completed the Training and Readiness (T&R) 4000 SSSP in its
entirety for that respective PMOS.
c.  Requirements.  Complete the respective type of engine test cell
training syllabus in Advanced Skills Management as per reference (ad)
COMNAVFORINST 4790.2 and (bn) NAVMC 4790.1.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ad) COMNAVFORINST 4790.2 and (bn) NAVMC 4790.1.

## 3138 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-358 Enclosure (1)
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Aircraft Mechanics and Service Technicians 49-3011.
f.  Related Military Skill
## (1) Helicopter Power Plants Mechanic, T-64, 6123.
(2) Helicopter Power Plants Mechanic, T-400/T-700, 6124.
(3) Fixed-Wing Power Plants Mechanic, F-402, 6222.
(4) Fixed-Wing Power Plants Mechanic, J-52, 6223.
(5) Fixed-Wing Power Plants Mechanic, T-56, 6226.
(6) Fixed-Wing Power Plants Mechanic, F-404, 6227.
-  MOS 6033, Aircraft Nondestructive Inspection Technician (GySgt to Cpl)
## NMOS (6062, 6092, 615X, 625X), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Aircraft Nondestructive Inspection Technicians perform
nondestructive testing of metals in aircraft structures and aircraft/engine
components.  This MOS will be assigned only to qualified Aircraft Structures
or Hydraulic Pneumatic Mechanics, MOSs 6062, 6092, 615X or 625X.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have a minimum of 30 months’ time in service.
(2) Must pass annual eye examination for nondestructive inspectors.
(3) Must possess one of the following PMOSs 6062, 6092, 615X, or
## 625X.
(4) Must have a minimum AFQT score of 55 or a minimum GT score of
## 105.
(5) Must pass ionizing radiation physical in accordance with NAVMEDP
## 5055.
(6) Must have a minimum of 12 months credibility in the rank of SSgt
to Cpl.
(7) Completed the Training and Readiness (T&R) 4000 SSSP for the
respective PMOS.
(8) Must have a minimum of 24 months obligated service upon
completion of the Naval Aircraft Nondestructive Inspection Technician Course.
c.  Requirements
(1) Complete the Naval Aviation Nondestructive Inspection Technician
Class C1 Course (N23C002).
(2) Respective nondestructive inspection technician qualification
syllabus within advanced skills management per reference (ad) COMNAVFORINST
4790.2 and (bn) NAVMC 4790.1.

## 3138 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-359 Enclosure (1)
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ad) COMNAVFORINST (4790.2) and (bn) NAVMC 4790.1.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code
## (1) Nuclear Technician 19-4051.
(2) Inspection, Testers, Sorters, Samplers, and Weighers 51A-9061.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 6035, Joint Strike Fighter (JSF) Maintenance Specialist (MGySgt to
Pvt) NMOS (6046, 6048, 6672, 6694), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Joint Strike Fighter Maintenance Specialists perform
specific support functions directly related to F-35 maintenance and the
autonomic logistics information system (ALIS).  This MOS will be assigned as
an NMOS only.
b.  Prerequisites.  Must be skill qualified as an Aviation Maintenance
Data Specialist (MOS 6046), Flight Equipment Technician (MOS 6048), Aviation
Supply Specialist (MOS 6672), or Aviation Logistics Information Management
System (ALIMS) Specialist (MOS 6694).  Refer to MOS specific Training and
## Readiness Manual.
c.  Requirements
(1) This MOS is to be assigned only as a necessary-PMOS to qualified
6046, 6048, 6672, and 6694 Marines who have successfully completed the
requisite training course, or the appropriate on the job training, in support
of F-35 maintenance and supply functions at the squadron level and are
designated by a squadron commanding officer.
(2) Aviation Maintenance Data Specialist, MOS 6046, must complete the
following course and be indirect support of F-35 operations Automated
Logistics Information System (ALIS) Database Administrators/Analyst Course
(N56F2M2), Eglin AFB, FL or mobile training team.
(3) Flight Equipment Technicians, MOS 6048, must complete the course
indicated, or its equivalent United Kingdom or Air Force course, and have
minimum 6 months MOS credibility in direct support of F-35 operations at the
squadron level, and be designated by the commanding officer.  Specific tasks
for MOS credibility can be found in the MOS training and readiness manual
must complete the F-35B/C Flight Equipment Organizational Maintenance Course
(N5694Q2), Eglin AFB, FL.
(4) Aviation Logistics Information Management System (ALIMS)
Specialist, MOS 6694, must complete the courses indicated to receive the NMOS
6035 designation.  Alternate means of attaining the 6035 NMOS requires a
minimum of 12 months MOS proficiency in direct support of F-35 operations and
be designated by the commanding officer.  Specific tasks for MOS proficiency
can be found in the MOS training and readiness manual:
(a) Complete the ALIS Administrator Course CID C-531-0038
## (N56F2L2).
(b) Complete the Security Plus Certification Course (M03D3RG,
M10D3RB, or M22D3R4).

## 3138 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-360 Enclosure (1)
(c) Complete the Network Plus Certification Course (M03KZ7G,
M10KZ7B, or M22KZ74).
(5) Aviation Supply Specialist, MOS 6672, must have minimum 6 months
MOS credibility in direct support of F-35 operations at the squadron level,
and be designated by the Commanding Officer.  Specific tasks for MOS
credibility can be found in the MOS Training and Readiness Manual.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to (bn),
NAVMC 4790.1 for MOS 6035.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code
(1) Stock Clerks and Order Fillers 43-5081 (6672).
(2) Computer Operators 43-9011 (6046 and 6694).
(3) Production, Planning, and Expediting Clerks 43-5061 (6046).
(4) Installation, Maintenance, and Repair Workers, All Other 49-9099
## (6048).
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 6042, Aviation Support Equipment Asset Manager (MGySgt to Pvt) PMOS,
## DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Aviation Support Equipment (SE) Asset Managers manage
assets under the cognizance of the aircraft maintenance material readiness
list (AMMRL) program, the F-35 lightning II program joint fleet management
plan, and the United States marine corps F-35 integrated sustainment plan.
They are responsible for SE physical and administrative inventory
accountability, fleet management, and requirements validation utilizing the
Department of Navy’s Support Equipment Management System (SEMS), and the F-35
lightning II joint program office’s lighting II support equipment management
system (LSEMS).  They manage SE assets at the organizational and intermediate
levels of maintenance via the O-level squadrons, marine aviation logistics
squadrons (MALS), marine aircraft wings (MAW), type commander (TYCOM) and
naval air systems command support equipment controlling authorities (SECA),
marine forces commands (MARFORs), and Headquarters Marine Corps (HQMC).  They
use logistical management techniques, supply chain procedures, technical
publications, automated data processing equipment to conduct, reconcile, and
report SE asset inventories; acquire and dispose of SE assets; train
subordinate SE asset managers, validate requirements, conduct liaison with
the SECA, and inform higher headquarters or adjacent commands on applicable
SE management policies or aviation logistics strategy and plans.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must be a U.S. citizen.
(2) Must have normal color perception.
(3) Must possess a CL score of 100 or higher.
(4) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.

## 3138 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-361 Enclosure (1)
c.  Requirements.  Complete the Individual Material Readiness List (IMRL)
Asset Manager Course (C-555-2020) (N3361U1) at NAS Meridian, MS.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ad) COMNAVFORINST 4790.2 and (bn) NAVMC 4790.1.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Information and Record Clerks, All Other 43-4199.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 6043, Aircraft Welder (GySgt to LCpl) NMOS (6092), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Aircraft Welders fabricate and repair aircraft metals
through basic welding using the gas/tungsten/titanium welding/arc/welding
(GTAW) process on aluminum alloys, steel alloys, stainless steel alloys, and
precipitating hardening nickel-based alloys.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Possess PMOS 6092.
(2) Must have normal color perception.
(3) Must have vision better than 20/30.
(4) Must have normal depth perception.
(5) Completed the Training and Readiness 4000 SSSP for PMOS 6092.
c.  Requirements
(1) Must complete one of the following:
(a) Aircraft/Support Equipment Basic Welding Certification Course
## (N-701-0007) (M0464QE).
(b) Oxyfuel/Shielded Metal Arc Welding Certification Course (N-
## 701-0009) (M04685E).
(2) Previously qualified individuals who need to recertify per
reference (ad) COMNAVFORINST 4790.2 must complete one of the following:
(a) Aircraft/Support Equipment Basic Welding Recertification
Course (N-701-0008) (M0464QE).
(b) Oxyfuel/Shielded Metal Arc Welder Recertification Course (N-
## 701-0010) (M04685E).
(3) The local administrative organization will assign MOS 6043 via
unit diary upon review of the Marine's graduation certificate from one of the
listed courses.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ad) COMNAVFORINST 4790.2 and (bn) 4790.1.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Welders, Cutters, Soldiers, and Brazers 51-4121.

## 3138 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-362 Enclosure (1)
f.  Related Military Skill.  Metal Worker, 1316.
-  MOS 6044, Additive Manufacturing Specialist (MSgt to Cpl) NMOS (2161,
## 6042, 6046, 6048, 6049, 6062, 6073, 6074, 6092, 6123, 6124, 6132, 6222, 6226,
## 6227, 6423, 6432, 6469, 6483, 6492, 6499, 6531, 6541, 6591, 6672, 6694), DC
## AVN
a.  Summary.  Additive Manufacturing Specialist provide additive
manufacturing capability to the Marine Aviation Logistics (MALS) units by
operating computer aided design software and various types of 3D printing
machines.  This MOS will be assigned as a NMOS.
b.  Prerequisites.  Must be designated as one of the following PMOSs
## 2161, 6042, 6046, 6048, 6049, 6062, 6073, 6074, 6092, 6123, 6124, 6132, 6222,
6226, 6227, 6423, 6432, 6469, 6483, 6492, 6499, 6531, 6541, 6591, 6672, or
## 6694.
c.  Requirements
(1) Complete the T&R level 1000 qualification.
(2) Must be associated with and actively maintaining the T&R
qualification requirements to perform AM duties for work center 550 over a
period of at least 9 months.
d.  Duties
(1) The duties and tasks will be as prescribed and designated in
references:
(a) Reference 4790.1:  Aviation Maintenance and Supply Training
and Readiness Program.
## (b) Reference 4790.2:  Naval Aviation Maintenance Program.
(c) Reference MCO P4790.1:  Maintenance Training and Management
## Evaluation Program.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code
(1) Commercial and Industrial Designers 27-1021 design and develop
manufactured products.
(2) Computer Occupations, Other 15-1299 computer aided design.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 6046, Aviation Maintenance Data Specialist (MGySgt to Pvt) PMOS, DC
## AVN
a.  Summary.  Aviation Maintenance Data Specialists maintain electronic
aeronautical records within a computerized maintenance management system.
They receive, compile, and record aeronautical component information from
maintenance work orders and aircraft diagnostic systems to provide up to date
information for maintenance scheduling.  This includes the receipt and/or
transfer of aircraft, engines, and tracked repairable components.  Screening
includes updating change management, engineering change proposals, and
modifications for applicability to assigned aeronautical equipment.

## 3138 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-363 Enclosure (1)
Responsibilities include tracking of naval aircraft flight record flight
information and smart aircraft portable memory devices to maintain up to date
usage for applicable equipment.  In addition, Aviation Maintenance Data
Specialist will initiate, maintain, close out, and reinitiate aeronautical
component records.  They maintain records on components that have service
life limits and will monitor accumulations of forced removal items per
engineering usage standards.  Aviation Maintenance Data Specialist are
required to prepare maintenance data reports and correspondence within
aircraft maintenance and repair activities.  They are also responsible for
trend interpretation/analysis in support of aviation readiness reporting
requirements to aid maintenance leadership and logisticians in the
performance of aviation maintenance related tasks.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must be a U.S. citizen.
(2) Must have normal color perception.
(3) Must possess an EL score of 100 or higher.
(4) Security Requirement:  must be able to obtain a secret security
clearance.
(5) Basic PC operation/word processing with the capability of 15
words per minute.
c.  Requirements.  Complete the appropriate formal "A" school, Aviation
Maintenance Administration Class A1 Course (N336441), Meridian, MS.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ad) COMNAVFORINST 4790.2 and (bn) NAVMC 4790.1.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Production, Planning, and Expediting Clerks 43-5061.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 6048, Flight Equipment Technician (GySgt to Pvt) PMOS, DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Flight Equipment Technicians inspect, maintain, and repair
parachutes, flight survival equipment, flight equipment, carbon dioxide, and
gaseous and liquid oxygen equipment.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have normal color perception.
(2) Must possess an MM score of 105 or higher.
(3) Must meet the medical requirements for Explosives Handlers
contained in reference (ak), Article 15-71B.
(4) Must meet the criteria to be fully qualified and certified as per
reference (ay), Qualification and Certification for Class V Munitions and
## Explosive Devices.


## 3138 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-364 Enclosure (1)
c.  Requirements
(1) Must complete the following course(s):
## (a) Aircrew Survival Equipment Common Core Class A1 Course
## (N23WSL2).
(b) Must meet the criteria to be fully qualified and certified as
per reference (ay), Qualification and Certification for Class V Munitions and
## Explosive Devices.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ad) COMNAVAIRFORINST 4790.2, (bn) NAVMC 4790.1, and (bo) NAVMC
## 3500.14.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Installation, Maintenance and Repair Workers, All Other 49-9099.
f.  Related Military Skill
(1) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Safety Equipment Mechanic, AV-8/TAV-8, 6282.
(2) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Safety Equipment Mechanic, KC-130, 6286.
(3) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Safety Equipment Mechanic, F/A-18, 6287.
(4) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Safety Equipment Mechanic, F-35B, 6288.
-  MOS 6049, Aviation Data Analyst (MGySgt to Sgt) NMOS (6046), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Aviation Data Analyst performs data analysis and trend
interpretation.  They provide qualitative and quantitative analytical
information to the Maintenance Department allowing a continuous review of the
management practices.  They establish, monitor, control, and apply the
maintenance data system or configuration management within the activity.
They serve as a contact point between work centers and the Naval Information
Warfare Center and is responsible for the management of all aspects of the
MDS to include NALCOMIS reports/inquiries at the activity level.  They have
full working knowledge of the principles of foundation, mid, and top tier
data replication and information available in the aviation data warehouse
(ADW).  They provide management with data, in graphic and narrative form,
necessary to make qualitative decisions about aircraft, equipment, test
bench, support equipment, material condition, readiness, utilization,
maintenance workload, or failure trends.  They ensure joint deficiency
reporting system, baseline trouble shooting report, baseline change report
and daily summary of changes reports are reviewed, and appropriate changes
are made to ensure the database is kept current.  Additional functions
include management of computer Information Systems, verification of database
integrity, control of user access to designated activity servers, monitoring
and ensuring system accuracy, supporting network system functions, accuracy
of database tables.  The Aviation Data Analyst is the acting authority for
the integrity and validity of all aviation readiness reporting data.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have a minimum of 6 months service remaining.

## 3138 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-365 Enclosure (1)
(2) Must be skill qualified as an Aircraft Maintenance Administration
Specialist (MOS 6046).
(3) Must have completed the training and readiness task 6046
OPTIMIZED ORG MAINT ACTV (OOMA) 2000-3000 SSSP (B) or training and readiness
task 6046 INTER MAINT ACTV Analyst (IMAA) 2000-3000 SSSP (B).
c.  Requirements
(1) Upon initial assignment to an IMA or OMA billet, complete one or
more the appropriate NALCOMIS Aviation Information Systems Administrator/
Analyst training tracks:
(a) NALCOMIS Aviation Maintenance IMA Database Administrator/
Analyst Pipeline Course (N23WTK2) at NATTC Pensacola, Fl.
(b) NALCOMIS Aviation Maintenance OMA Database Administrator/
Analyst Pipeline Course (N23WTH2) At NATTC Pensacola, Fl.
(c) Network Plus Certification Course (M03KZ7G, M10KZ7B, or
## M22KZ74).
(d) Security Plus Certification Course (M03D3RG, M10D3RB, or
## M22D3R4).
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ad) COMNAVFORINST 4790.2 and (bn) NAVMC 4790.1.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code
## (1) Computer Operators 43-9011.
(2) Production, Planning, and Expediting Clerks 43-5061.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 6053, CH 53K Maintenance Specialist (GySgt to Pvt) NMOS (6113, 6153,
## 6173, 6323), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  This MOS is to be assigned only as a necessary-PMOS (NMOS)
to qualified Marines who have successfully completed the requisite training
course, or the appropriate on the job training, in support of CH-53K
maintenance functions at the squadron level.
b.  Prerequisites.  Must be qualified in the following Primary MOS 6113,
6153, 6173, 6323.  Refer to MOS specific Training and Readiness Manual for
Qualification/Certification/Licensing requirements.
c.  Requirements
(1) This MOS is to be assigned only as a necessary-PMOS (NMOS) to
qualified Marines who have successfully completed the requisite training
course, or the appropriate on the job training, in support of CH-53K
maintenance functions at the squadron level.  Specific MOS requirements are
as follows:
(a) West Palm Beach detachment maintenance personnel who
demonstrate the requisite understanding of CH-53K theory of operation and

## 3138 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-366 Enclosure (1)
maintenance proficiency may be designated by the CH53K Operational Test
## Director.
(b) Successfully complete a HQMC delineated OEM designated and be
designated by the CNATTMARU Commanding Officer.
(c) Successful completion the 1000 LVL T&R.
d.  Duties.  For a listing of duties and tasks, refer to reference (bo),
Aviation Training and Readiness Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## None.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 6062, Aircraft Intermediate Level Hydraulic/Pneumatic Mechanic
(GySgt to Pvt) PMOS, DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Aircraft Intermediate Level Hydraulic/Pneumatic Mechanics
inspect, maintain, and repair aircraft intermediate level hydraulic/pneumatic
system components.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have normal color perception.
(2) Must possess a CL score of 105 or MM score of 105 or higher.
c.  Requirements
(1) Must complete the following courses:
(a) Aviation Structural Mechanic Core Course (N23WSG2).
## (b) Aircraft Hydraulic Components Intermediate Maintenance Course
## (N25WRR1).
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ad) COMNAVAIRFORINST 4790.2, (bn) NAVMC 4790.1, and (bo) NAVMC
## 3500.14.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Aircraft Mechanics and Service Technicians 49-3011.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 6073, Support Equipment Electrician/Refrigeration and Engine/Gas
Turbine Technician (GySgt to Pvt) PMOS, DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Support Equipment Electrician/Refrigeration and Engine/Gas
Turbine Technicians inspect, test, maintain, and repair aircraft support
equipment (SE), electrical/refrigeration and engine/gas turbine systems and
systems components.  Perform duties related to the operation of support
equipment.  Perform duties related to the licensing of aircraft maintenance
personnel in the operation of support/special support equipment.


## 3138 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-367 Enclosure (1)
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have normal color perception.
(2) Must possess an EL score of 105 or MM score of 105 or higher.
c.  Requirements
(1) Must complete the following courses:
## (a) Aviation Support Equipment Technical Class A1 Course
## (N236482).
(b) Support Equipment Electrical/Refrigeration Intermediate
Maintenance Course (N28WPU7 or N37WPU2).
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ad) COMNAVAIRFORINST 4790.2, (bn) NAVMC 4790.1, and (bo) NAVMC
## 3500.14.

## (1) Mobile Heavy Equipment Mechanics, Except Engines 49-3042.
(2) Bus and Truck Mechanics, and Diesel Engine Specialists 49-3031.
(3) Heating Air Conditioner and Refrigeration Mechanics and
## Installers 49-9021.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 6074, Cryogenics Equipment Operator (GySgt to Pvt) PMOS, DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Cryogenics Equipment Operators assemble, operate, and
maintain liquid oxygen/nitrogen generating plants, storage and aircraft
servicing equipment, vaporizing equipment, vacuum pumps, and LOX tank purging
units.  They operate and maintain purity analysis test equipment to ensure
product acceptability.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have normal color perception.
(2) Must possess a MM score of 95 or higher.
c.  Requirements.  Must complete the formal school, Air Separation and
Cryogenics Generators and Servicing Equipment Intermediate Maintenance Course
## (M041366).
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ad) COMNAVAIRFORINST 4790.2, (bn) NAVMC 4790.1, and (bo) NAVMC
## 3500.14.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Chemical Plant and System Operators 51-8091.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.

## 3138 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-368 Enclosure (1)

## PMOS, DC AVN

maintain, and repair aircraft intermediate level structural components.

-  MOS 6113, Helicopter Mechanic, CH-53 (GySgt to Pvt) PMOS, DC AVN

repair power plants, transmissions, drivetrains, fuel systems, flight control
systems, and rotor systems.
b.  Prerequisites

(2) Must pass the normal color perception test (no waivers).
(3) Must possess a CL score of 105 or MM score of 105 or higher.

Systems Course (M0590N1).

reference(s) (ad) COMNAVAIRFORINST 4790.2 and (bn) NAVMC 4790.1.

## 49-1011.

b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have five year term of enlistment.
(2) Must pass the normal color perception test (no waivers).
(3) Must possess an EL score of 105 or MM score of 105 or higher.
c.  Requirements.  Complete the V-22 Tiltrotor Mechanics (Osprey) Course
## (M0568E1).

reference(s) (ad) COMNAVAIRFORINST 4790.2 and (bn) NAVMC 4790.1.

(1) First-Line Supervisors of Mechanics, Installers, and Repairers
## 49-1011.
(2) Aircraft Mechanics and Service Technicians 49-3011.
(3) Cleaners of Vehicles and Equipment 53-7061.

(1) Helicopter Mechanic, CH-53, 6113.
(2) Helicopter Mechanic, UH/AH-1, 6114.
(3) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Mechanic AV-8/TAV-8, 6212.

(6) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Mechanic F-35, 6218.
-  MOS 6122, Helicopter Power Plants Mechanic, T-58 (GySgt to Pvt) PMOS, DC
## AVN
a.  Summary.  THIS IS AN OBSOLETE MOS, NO LONGER IN USE IN THE TABLE OF
## ORGANIZATION OF THE MARINE CORPS.  THIS MOS IS MAINTAINED IN THE MOS MANUAL
## BECAUSE IT APPEARS IN THE RECORDS OF MARINES STILL ON ACTIVE DUTY.
Helicopter Power Plants Mechanics, T-58, inspect, maintain, test, repair, and
perform complete repair of helicopter power plants and power plants systems.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have normal color perception.
(2) Must possess an MM score of 105 or higher.
c.  Requirements.  Complete the appropriate formal "A" school, Aviation
Machinist Mate Common Core Course (N236982); and the Aviation Machinist Mate
Helicopter Fundamentals Strand Course (N236992) at NATTC, NAS Pensacola, FL.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (bn), Maintenance Training Management and Evaluation Programs
(MATMEP) for MOS 6122.

-  MOS 6140, Fixed-Wing Aircraft Loadmaster, C-40A (SSgt to LCpl) NMOS
## (6276), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Fixed-Wing Aircraft Loadmaster, C-40A; are mission
essential flight crew members who oversee cargo loading and passenger
embarkation operations aboard the C-40A, assist with performance
calculations, and liaise with airfield and customs authorities for the
efficient conduct of air operations.  This MOS will be assigned as an NMOS.
b.  Prerequisites.   For a complete listing of prerequisites, refer to
the USMC C-40A Training and Readiness Manual.
c.  Requirements
(1) Complete the training requirements for the C-40A Loadmaster
designation, in accordance with the USMC C-40A Training and Readiness Manual.
(2) Be designated, in writing by the commanding officer, as a
qualified C-40A Loadmaster.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to the
USMC C-40A Training and Readiness Manual.

servicing functions for C-40A operations.  This MOS will be assigned as an
## NMOS.

designation, in accordance with the USMC C-40A Training and Readiness Manual.

qualified C-40A Crew Chief.

USMC C-40A Training and Readiness Manual.

-  MOS 6171, Night Systems Instructor (NSI) Enlisted Aircrew (MGySgt to
LCpl) NMOS (6173, 6174, 6176, 6276, 7372), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Night Systems Instructor (NSI) Enlisted Aircrew are
responsible for the initial and refresher training of unit aircrew, focusing
on the utilization of Night Vision Devices (NVDs) during nighttime
operations.  They assist the unit’s Weapons and Tactics Instructors (WTI) in
the continual training of enlisted aircrew in order to ensure the unit’s
deployment readiness status, capable of accomplishing its assigned Mission
Essential Tasks (METS).
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must possess MOSs 6173, 6174, 6176, 6276, or 7372.
(2) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.
c.  Requirements.  Must successfully complete the NSI syllabus for their
respective type model series in accordance with the Marine Aviation Weapons
and Tactics Squadron One (MAWTS-1) Course Catalog.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to the
Training and Readiness (T&R) manual for the respective TMS in accordance with
reference (bo) NAVMC 3500.14.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Teachers and Instructors, All Other, Except Substitute Teachers, 25-3097.
f.  Related Military Skill.  Weapons and Tactics Instructor, 6177.
-  MOS 6173, Helicopter Crew Chief, CH-53 (GySgt to Pvt) PMOS, DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Helicopter Crew Chiefs, CH-53; are flight crew members who,
when assigned to the flight schedule, are responsible to the pilot in command
while in operation of the aircraft and associated mission/weapons systems.

## 3139 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-378 Enclosure (1)
When not assigned to the flight schedule they report to their respective work
center within the maintenance department where they inspect, service,
maintain, and repair power plants, transmissions, drivetrains, fuel systems,
flight control systems, and rotor systems.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must be a U.S. citizen (no waivers).
(2) Must pass a rigorous flight physical.
(3) Must have a five year term of enlistment.
(4) Must have WS-B(+) water survival qualification.
(5) Must pass the normal color perception test (no waivers).
(6) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.
(7) Volunteer for duties involving flight as an aircraft crewmember.
(8) Meet the requirements of reference (ah), and (ai) with normal
depth perception.
(9) Must possess an EL score of 105 or GT score of 105 or MM score of
105 or higher.
(10) Vision no worse than 20/70 in one eye and 20/100 in the other
with overall vision correctable to 20/20.
c.  Requirements
(1) Must complete the following formal course(s):
(a) CH-53E Crew Chief Training Syllabus Course (M05AG21).
(b) Naval Air Crewmember Candidate School Course (N2373C3).
(c) CH-53E Marine Enlisted Aircrew Training Course (M05AFA3).
(d) Marines who completed all requirements listed above in FY20
and later without completing Survival, Evasion, Resistance, and Escape Course
are considered MOS qualified.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference(s) (ad) COMNAVAIRFORINST 4790.2, (bn) NAVMC 4790.1, and (bo) NAVMC
## 3500.14.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code
(1) First-Line Supervisors of Mechanics, Installers, and Repairers
## 49-1011.
(2) Aircraft Mechanics and Service Technicians 49-3011.
## (3) Aircraft Cargo Handling Supervisor 53-1041.
(4) First-Line Supervisors of Air Crew Members 55-2011.

## 3139 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-379 Enclosure (1)
(5) Cleaners of Vehicles and Equipment 53-7061.
## (6) Air Crew Members 55-3011.

(1) Helicopter Mechanic, UH/AH-1, 6114.
(2) Tiltrotor Mechanic, MV-22, 6116.
(3) Helicopter Crew Chief, UH-1, 6174.

-  MOS 6177, Weapons and Tactics Crew Chief Instructor (MGySgt to LCpl)
## NMOS (6173, 6174, 6176, 6276, 7372), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Weapons and Tactics Instructors (WTI) are the senior
instructors, for all Crew Chiefs (CCs) and Aerial Observers (AOs) within
their squadron.  They are familiarized with Marine Air Ground Task Force
(MAGTF) concepts, planning, and operations, with a focus on the integration
of their specific type model series (TMS) aircraft during operations in all
environmental and threat conditions.  They are familiarized with adult
learning theory and various learning taxonomies e.g. Blooms, Finks, SOLO,
etc.  They instruct everything from basic level aircraft, weapons, and
mission’s systems knowledge and employment to advanced level tactics,
techniques, and procedures, for their specific TMS along with MAGTF concepts
and integration.  They train and evaluate Marines for basic instructor
qualifications and prepare them for advanced instructor qualifications
evaluated by Marine Aviation Weapon and Tactics Squadron One (MAWTS-1).  WTIs
are capable of creating and executing a full spectrum training plan to ensure
the squadron’s readiness level for the accomplishment of their assigned
mission essential task list (METL).  As such, any WTI is able to be assigned
as the Enlisted Aircrew Training Manager (EATM) for the squadron.  The
squadron Maintenance chief with the help of the line division chief and
senior ranking WTI Crew Chief will assign an EATM to the operations
department where they will report directly to the operations officer.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Security requirement:  secret security clearance.
(2) Must possess one of the following primary MOSs 6173, 6174, 6176,
6276, or 7372.
c.  Requirements.  Must complete the following formal course Weapons and
Tactics Instructor (WTI) Crew Chief/Enlisted Aircrew Course (M149741).
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ad) COMNAVAIRFORINST 4790.2, (bn) NAVMC 4790.1, and TMS specific
T&R and MAWTS-1 course catalog per reference (bo) NAVMC 3500.14.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Teachers and Instructors, All Other, Except Substitute Teachers, 25-3097.

## 3139 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-383 Enclosure (1)
f.  Related Military Skill.  Night Systems Instructor, Enlisted Aircrew,
## 6171.
-  MOS 6178, VH-60N Presidential Helicopter Crew Chief (MGySgt to LCpl)
NMOS (any PMOS from 60/61/62/63/64/65), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Presidential Helicopter Crew Chiefs are flight crewmembers
that perform duties incident to the maintenance and operation of Marine VH-
60N Helicopters of the Executive Flight Detachment at HMX-1.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have normal color perception.
(2) Must have WS-B(+) water survival qualification.
(3) Volunteer for duties involving flight as an aircraft crewmember.
(4) Security requirement:  top secret security clearance with yankee
white (YW) access.
c.  Requirements
(1) Meet the requirements of reference (ah), and reference (ai) with
normal depth perception.
(2) Skill qualified in a primary MOS from any of the following
OccFlds 60/61, Aircraft Maintenance, 63/64, Avionics, 65, Aviation Ordnance.
(3) Satisfactory performance in primary MOS duties for the initial 6
months of assignment to the HMX-1 Executive Flight Detachment.
(4) Must complete the Plane Captain (PC) Ground Syllabus for type
aircraft.
(5) Complete all requirements contained in appropriate NATOPS manual
or syllabi peculiar to the VH-60N helicopter.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (bn), Maintenance Training Management and Evaluation Programs
(MATMEP) for MOS 6178.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## Air Crew Members 55-3011.
f.  Related Military Skill
(1) Helicopter Crew Chief, CH-53, 6173.
(2) Helicopter Crew Chief, UH/AH-1, 6174.
(3) Tiltrotor Crew Chief, MV-22, 6176.




## 3139 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-384 Enclosure (1)
-  MOS 6181, VH-92 Presidential Helicopter Crew Chief (MGySgt to LCpl) NMOS
(any PMOS from 60/61/62/63/64/65), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  VH-XX Presidential Helicopter Crew Chiefs are flight
crewmembers that perform duties incident to the maintenance and operation of
Marine VH-XX Helicopters of the Executive Flight Detachment at HMX-1.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have normal color perception.
(2) Must have WS-B(+) water survival qualification.
(3) Must volunteer for duties involving flight as an aircraft
crewmember.
(4) Must be skill qualified in a PMOS from OccFld 60, 61, 62, 63, 64,
or 65.
(5) Security requirement:  top secret security clearance eligibility
with yankee white (YW) access.
c.  Requirements
(1) Meet the requirements of reference (ah), and the reference (ai),
USN Manual of the Medical Department with normal depth perception.
(2) Satisfactory performance in primary MOS duties for the initial 6
months of assignment to the HMX-1 Executive Flight Detachment.
(3) Must complete the Plane Captain (PC) Ground Syllabus for type
aircraft.
(4) MOS awarded upon certification by CO HMX-1 to MMOA that Marine
has completed all requirements contained in appropriate NATOPS manual or
syllabi peculiar to the VH-XX helicopter.
d.  Duties.  For a listing of duties and tasks, refer to reference (bn),
Maintenance Training Management and Evaluation Programs (MATMEP).
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## None.
f.  Related Military Skill
(1) Helicopter Crew Chief, CH-53, 6173.
(2) Helicopter Crew Chief, UH/AH-1, 6174.
(3) Tiltrotor Crew Chief, MV-22, 6176.
-  MOS 6199, Enlisted Aircrew/Aerial Observer/Gunner (MGySgt to Pvt) FMOS,
## DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Enlisted Aircrew/Aerial Observers/ Aerial Gunners are
flight crew members who, when assigned to the flight schedule, are
responsible to the pilot in command to assist the crew chief while in
operation of the aircraft and associated mission/weapons systems.  Their

## 3139 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-385 Enclosure (1)
duties include but are not limited to; assist in operational planning, assist
in pre-flight/post flight of the aircraft, assist in start-up/shut-down of
the aircraft, assist in various ground taxi/take-off/in-flight/landing
duties, obstacle detection/avoidance, navigational assistance, threat
detection/avoidance, embark/debark and securing of passengers/cargo,
operation of mission/weapons systems, liaison between pilots and ground
personnel, identify and diagnosis in-flight aircraft/mission/weapons systems
malfunctions, and assist in execution of emergency procedures.  They are
required to employ crew concepts and utilize Crew Resource Management in the
performance of their duties.  Strong verbal communications are required in
the performance of their duties.  They are required to understand the theory
and operation of all aircraft/mission/weapons systems in order to provide
assistance to identify, diagnosis, execute emergency procedures.  They
perform a multitude of ground handling duties in support of flight operations
e.g. taxi directing of aircraft, ground refueling, aircraft towing, securing/
un-securing of aircraft for flight, assist in pre-flight/post flight of
aircraft, etc.  Strong verbal communication skills are needed in the
performance of their duties.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have normal color perception.
(2) Must have WS-B(+) water survival qualification.
(3) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.
(4) Volunteer for duties involving flight as an aircraft crewmember.
c.  Requirements
(1) Meet the requirements of reference (ah) and reference (ai), the
USN Manual of the Medical Department with normal depth perception.
(2) Complete all requirement contained in appropriate NATOPS Manual
or syllabi peculiar to the aircraft.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (bo), Aviation Training and Readiness Programs.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## Air Crew Members 55-3011.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.

## 3140 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-386 Enclosure (1)
## 3140.  OCCUPATIONAL FIELD 62, AIRCRAFT MAINTENANCE (FIXED-WING)
-  MOS 6212, Fixed-Wing Aircraft Mechanic, AV-8/TAV-8 (GySgt to Pvt) PMOS,
## DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Fixed-Wing Aircraft Mechanics, AV-8/TAV-8 inspect, service,
maintain, and repair aircraft airframes, airframe components, power plants,
transmissions, fuel systems, and perform duties relating to flight line
operations.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must be a U.S. citizen.
(2) Must have normal color vision.
(3) Must have a five year term of enlistment.
(4) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.
(5) Must possess a CL score of 105 or MM score of 105 or higher.
c.  Requirements
(1) Must complete the following course(s):
(a) Aviation Machinist Mate Common Core Class Course (N236982).
(b) AV-8 Aircraft Mechanic Organizational Maintenance Course
## (M046946).
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference(s) (ad) COMNAVAIRFORINST 4790.2 and (bn) NAVMC 4790.1.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code
(1) First-Line Supervisors of Mechanics, Installers, and Repairers
## 49-1011.
(2) Aircraft Mechanics and Service Technicians 49-3011.
(3) Cleaners of Vehicles and Equipment 53-7061.
f.  Related Military Skill
(1) Helicopter Mechanic, CH-53, 6113.
(2) Helicopter Mechanic, UH/AH-1, 6114.
(3) Tiltrotor Mechanic, MV-22, 6116.
(4) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Mechanic KC-130, 6216.
(5) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Mechanic F-18, 6217.
(6) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Mechanic F-35, 6218.


## 3140 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-387 Enclosure (1)
-  MOS 6214, Unmanned Aircraft Mechanic, MQ-9 (GySgt to Pvt) PMOS, DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Unmanned Aircraft Mechanic, inspect, service, maintain, and
repair aircraft airframes, airframes components, airframe structures,
composite material, power plants, transmissions, fuel systems, pneumatic
systems, hydraulic systems, landing gear systems, and perform duties relating
to flight line operations.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must have normal color perception.
(2) Must have a five-year term enlistment.
(3) Must possess an MM score of 105 or higher.
(4) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.
c.  Requirements.  The 62xx OccFld sponsor (ASB-33C) will award the 6214
PMOS to Marines after reviewing necessary qualifications to fulfill initial
qualifiers for Unmanned Aircraft Systems Mechanics.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference(s) (ad) COMNAVAIRFORINST 4790.2 and (bn) NAVMC 4790.1.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## None.
f.  Related Military Skill
(1) Helicopter Mechanic, CH-53, 6113.
(2) Helicopter Mechanic, UH/AH-1, 6114.
(3) Tiltrotor Mechanic, MV-22, 6116.
(4) Helicopter Airframe Mechanic, CH-53, 6153.
(5) Helicopter Airframe Mechanic, UH/AH-1, 6154.
(6) Tiltrotor Airframe Mechanic, MV-22, 6156.
(7) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Mechanic, AV-TV-8, 6212.
(8) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Mechanic, KC-130, 6216.
(9) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Mechanic, F/A-18, 6217.
(10) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Mechanic, F-35, 6218.
(11) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Airframe Mechanic, AV-TV-8, 6252.
(12) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Airframe Mechanic, KC-130, 6256.
(13) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Airframe Mechanic, F/A-18, 6257.
(14) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Airframe Mechanic, F-35, 6258.

-  MOS 6242, Fixed-Wing Aircraft Flight Engineer, KC-130 (MGySgt to Sgt)
## NMOS (6276), DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Fixed-Wing Aircraft Flight Engineers, KC-130; monitor all
aircraft systems and operate the flight engineer’s panel during flight
operations.  Assist the pilot/copilot in preflight and post flight
inspections of the aircraft, supervise servicing functions and record all
discrepancies upon completion of flight.

## 3140 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-393 Enclosure (1)
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.
(2) Must be designated as PMOS 6276, Fixed-Wing Aircraft Loadmaster,
## KC-130.
c.  Requirements
(1) Complete training requirements for the KC-130 Flight Engineer
designation, in accordance with the KC-130 Loadmaster Training and Readiness
Manual, per reference (bo) NAVMC 3500.14.
(2) Designated as a KC-130 Flight Engineer by the commanding officer,
in accordance with the KC-130 Loadmaster Training and Readiness Manual, per
reference (bo) NAVMC 3500.14.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ad) COMVANFORINST 4790.2, (bn) NAVMC 4790.1, and (bo) NAVMC
## 3500.14.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code
(1) Aircraft Mechanics and Service Technicians 49-3011.
(2) Aircraft Structure, Surfaces, Rigging, and Systems Assemblers 51-
## 2011.
(3) Airline Pilots, Copilots, and Flight Engineers 53-2011.

(1) Helicopter Crew Chief, CH-53, 6173.
(2) Helicopter Crew Chief, UH-1, 6174.
(3) Tiltrotor Crew Chief, MV-22, 6176.

responsible to perform duties incident to the maintenance and operation of
Marine C-20 Fixed-Wing Transport Aircraft at VR-1.  This MOS will be assigned
as a NMOS only.

(1) Must volunteer for aircrew training.
(2) Must have skill qualified in a primary MOS from any of the
following OccFlds 60/61/62 Aircraft Maintenance or 63/64 Avionics.

(1) Satisfactory performance in primary MOS duties for the
initial six months of assignment to VMR-1.

Structural Mechanic Core Course (N23WSG2), may attend the F-35 Aircraft

-  MOS 6276, Fixed-Wing Aircraft Loadmaster, KC-130 (MGySgt to Pvt) PMOS,
## DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Fixed-Wing Aircraft Loadmaster, KC-130; are mission
essential flight crew members who are responsible for aircraft preparation
and configuration prior to, during, after and between missions.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must be a U.S. citizen.
(2) Must have normal color perception.
(3) Must have 36 months obligated services.
(4) Must have WS-B(+) water survival qualification.
(5) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.
(6) Volunteer for duties involving flight as an aircraft crewmember.
(7) Must possess an EL score of 105 or GT score of 105 or MM score of
105 or higher.
(8) Must be physically qualified and aeronautically adapted for all
duties involving flying as a Naval Aircrewman as required by the Manual of
the Medical Department, U.S. Navy with normal depth perception.


## 3140 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-399 Enclosure (1)
c.  Requirements
(1) Complete the Naval Air Crewman Candidate School Course (N237C3).
(2) Complete the KC-130J Loadmaster Initial Accession Aircrew Course
(M0460H2 or M1760H5).
(3) Active duty must have 36 months obligated service time while
reserve Marines must have 180 days of active duty time remaining upon
completion of the Formal Course (M0460H2 or M1760H5).
(4) Marines who completed all requirements listed above in FY20 and
later without completing Survival, Evasion, Resistance, and Escape Course are
considered MOS qualified.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (ad) COMNAVAIRFORINST 4790.2, (bn) NAVMC 4790.1, and (bo) NAVMC
## 3500.14.

(1) Transportation, Storage, and Distribution Managers, 11-3071.
## (2) Aircraft Cargo Handling Supervisors, 53-1041.

(5) First-Line Supervisors of All Other Tactical Operations, 55-2013.

(1) Fixed-Wing Aircraft Mechanic, KC-130, 6216.
(2) Fixed-Wing Transport Aircraft Specialist, C-20, 6246.

## 3141 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-404 Enclosure (1)
## 3141.  OCCUPATIONAL FIELD 63, ORGANIZATIONAL AVIONICS MAINTENANCE
-  Introduction.  The avionics OccFld includes direct and indirect support
of all aviation weapon systems.  While there is a large similarity in the
skills required to provide this support, the systems being supported are
diverse.  The following examples are provided:  direct support repair
accomplished at the organizational maintenance activity (OMA) which is
normally accomplished on aircraft, e.g., replacement of the radar antenna on
the aircraft, indirect support-repair accomplished at the intermediate
maintenance activity (IMA) which is normally accomplished off aircraft, e.g.,
radar system module repair, and repair/calibration of the radar support
equipment.  Marines entering the OccFld receive MOS 6300, Basic Avionics
Marine, and then progress through specific hard skill MOSs and ultimately are
assigned MOS 6391, Avionics Chief.  Qualifications initially include
technical proficiency in the repair of one or more electronic systems after
completion of formal training.  As the Marine is promoted, repair and
administrative requirements for multiple systems take on an equal importance
until the Marine is ultimately placed in a supervisory position.  Billets for
both repair and administration are varied and extend from the squadron level
to staff positions at the Wing and force commander, and Headquarters U.S.
Marine Corps level.
-  MOS 6313, Aircraft Communications/Navigation/Radar Systems Technician,
EA-6 (GySgt to Pvt) PMOS, DC AVN
a.  Summary.  THIS IS AN OBSOLETE MOS, NO LONGER IN USE IN THE TABLE OF
## ORGANIZATION OF THE MARINE CORPS.  THIS MOS IS MAINTAINED IN THE MOS MANUAL
BECAUSE IT APPEARS IN THE RECORDS OF MARINES STILL ON ACTIVE DUTY.  Aircraft
Communications/Navigation/Radar Systems Technicians, EA-6 install, remove,
inspect, test, maintain and repair systems components, and ancillary
equipment of installed aircraft communications/navigation/electrical/radar
systems to include deceptive electronic countermeasures (DECM) systems at the
OMA level.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must be a U.S. citizen.
(2) Must have normal color perception.
(3) Must possess an EL score of 105 or higher.
(4) Security Requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.
c.  Requirements
(1) Complete the Avionics Technician O Level Class A1 Course
(N23A962) at NATTC NAS Pensacola, FL.
(2) Complete the EA-6B ICAP 2/Block 86 COMM/NAV/RADAR/ECM Systems
Organization Maintenance Course (N2694B2) at CNATT Unit Whidbey Island, WA.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (bn), Aviation Maintenance and Supply Training and Readiness
Program (AMSTRP) for MOS 6313.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## Avionics Technicians 49-2091.

## 3141 NAVMC 1200.1L

(UAS) install, remove, inspect, test, maintain and repair systems components,
and ancillary equipment.  These Marines also perform launch recovery and
other flight line operations at the organizational maintenance level.

qualifiers for Avionics/Maintenance Technicians, Unmanned Aircraft Systems
## (UAS).

Technician Course (N56A392), “C” School, CNATT Unit Eglin AFB, FL.
(3) LATMOV Marines from MOS within 63XX/64XX occupational fields but
not TACAIR MOSs (6314/6316/6323/6324/6326/6336/6423/6432/6469/6483/6492/
6499) do not need to attend “A” School.  As long as the Marine has previously
completed either ATI (N23A952), ATO (N23A962), or AE (N23A972).  Must
complete the F-35B Aircraft Avionics Systems Technician Course (N56A392), “C”
School, CNATT Unit Eglin AFB, FL.
(4) E3-LATMOV Marines from MOSs within the 63XX occupational field
with TACAIR MOSs 6317/6332/ 6337; do not need to attend “A” School.  As long
as the Marine has previously completed either ATI (N23A952), ATO (N23A962),
or AE (N23A972).  Must complete the F-35B Aircraft Avionics Systems
Technician Course (N56A392), “C” School, CNATT Unit Eglin AFB, FL.
(5) E4 and above-LATMOV Marines from the 63XX occupational field with
TACAIR MOSs 6317/6332/6337 do not need to attend “A” School.  As long as the
Marine has previously completed either ATI (N23A952), ATO (N23A962), or AE
(N23A972).  Must complete either “C” School (N56A392), CNATT Unit Eglin AFB,
FL or Fleet Training Detachment FTD VMFAT-501 (M16A391) or VMFAT-502
## (M17A39C).

## 3-412 Enclosure (1)

reference (bn), Aviation Maintenance and Supply Training and Readiness

-  MOS 6386, Aircraft Electronic Countermeasures Systems Technician, EA-6B
(GySgt to Pvt) PMOS, DC AVN
a.  Summary.  THIS IS AN OBSOLETE MOS, NO LONGER IN USE IN THE TABLE OF
## ORGANIZATION OF THE MARINE CORPS.  THIS MOS IS MAINTAINED IN THE MOS MANUAL
BECAUSE IT APPEARS IN THE RECORDS OF MARINES STILL ON ACTIVE DUTY.  Aircraft
Electronic Countermeasures Systems Technicians install, remove, inspect,
test, maintain and repair system components, and ancillary equipment of
installed aircraft electronic countermeasures systems at the OMA level.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must be a U.S. citizen.
(2) Must have normal color perception.
(3) Must possess an EL score of 105 or higher.
(4) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.
c.  Requirements
(1) Complete the Avionics Technician O Level Class A1 Course
(N23A962) at NATTC NAS Pensacola, FL.
(2) Complete the EA-6B ECM Initial Organizational Maintenance Course
(N26EA62) at CNATT Unit NAS Whidbey Island, WA.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (bn), Aviation Maintenance and Supply Training and Readiness
Program (AMSTRP) for MOS 6386.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## Avionics Technicians 49-2091.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 6391, Avionics Chief (MGySgt to MSgt) PMOS, DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Avionics Chiefs, as staff noncommissioned officers in
charge of the avionics department supervise the maintenance and repair of
aircraft avionics systems, equipment and components at the IMA/OMA levels.
At the intermediate maintenance activity (MALS), the Avionics Chief (MGySgt)
serves as the OccFld sponsor for all 63/6400 MOS within the MAG.  At the
organizational maintenance activity, the Avionics Chief holds the

## 3141 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-413 Enclosure (1)
responsibility of managing the tier III EKMS account/assets as well as
manages avionics manpower within the unit.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must be a U.S. citizen.
(2) Must have normal color perception.
(3) Must possess an EL score of 105 or higher.
(4) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.
c.  Requirements
(1) Must satisfy the requirements for one of the PMOSs within the
6300 or 6400 OccFlds.
(2) Upon promotion to the rank of master sergeant, should attend and
successfully complete the Avionics Officer/Avionics Chief Foundation Course
(N2362X2) at NAS Pensacola, FL.
d.  Duties
(1) MGySgt and MSgt:
(a) Provides subject matter expertise to appropriate 63XX and
64XX lower level MOSs and input on career progression of all Marines in their
charge.
(b) Demonstrates a thorough working knowledge of COMNAVAIRFORINST
4790.2 and 3M procedures.
(c) Ensures facilities are organized to support the maintenance
and repair of avionics systems, equipment, or components at the IMA/OMA
levels.
(d) Supervise the requisition of spare/replacement parts and
necessary work center supplies and equipment using publications and supply
documents.
(e) Maintains appropriate records of all maintenance and
inspection actions performed, technical training conducted, and organic
equipment assigned to the avionics department.
(f) Possesses a thorough understanding and working knowledge of
sound management principles, work center supervision, proper methods of
training personnel, and accepted and approved avionics maintenance
procedures.
(g) Pursues Defense Acquisition Workforce Improvement (DAWIA)
Certification in order to gain qualifications prior to entering senior
billets within the MOS.  Marines serving in billets coded with an additional
skill designator (ASD) MOS 8060 as per the table of organization must attain
the appropriate certification level through the Defense Acquisition
University (DAU) within 24 months of billet assignment.  The three primary
areas of study typically applicable to AVLOG are:

## 3141 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-414 Enclosure (1)
-   Life Cycle Logistics (LCL).
-   Production, Quality, Manufacturing (PQM).
-   Program Management (PM).
(2) MGySgt:
(a) Possesses a thorough understanding and working knowledge of
Marine Corps administrative procedures including the format, preparation, and
submission of naval messages.
(b) Demonstrates a thorough understanding of Marine Corps
aviation staff organization and functioning of the Marine Aviation Logistic
Squadron, Marine Aircraft Wing, and FMF Headquarters levels.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
First-line Supervisors/Managers of Mechanics, Installers, and Repairers 49-
## 1011.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.

## 3142 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-415 Enclosure (1)
## 3142.  OCCUPATIONAL FIELD 64, INTERMEDIATE AVIONICS MAINTENANCE
-  Introduction.  The avionics OccFld includes direct and indirect support
of all aviation weapon systems.  While there is a large similarity in the
skills required to provide this support, the systems being supported are
diverse.  The following examples are provided:  direct support repair
accomplished at the organizational maintenance activity (OMA) which is
normally accomplished on aircraft, e.g., replacement of the radar antenna on
the aircraft; indirect support-repair accomplished at the intermediate
maintenance activity (IMA) which is normally accomplished off aircraft, e.g.,
radar system module repair, and repair/calibration of the radar support
equipment.  Marines entering the OccFld receive MOS 6300, Basic Avionics
Marine, and then progress through specific hard skill MOSs and ultimately are
assigned MOS 6391, Avionics Chief.  Qualifications initially include
technical proficiency in the repair of one or more electronic systems after
completion of formal training.  As the Marine is promoted, repair and
administrative requirements for multiple systems take on an equal importance
until the Marine is ultimately placed in a supervisory position.  Billets for
both repair and administration are varied and extend from the squadron level
to staff positions at the Wing and force commander, and Headquarters U.S.
Marine Corps level.
-  MOS 6423, Aviation Electronic Micro/Miniature Component and Cable Repair
Technician, IMA (GySgt to Pvt) PMOS, DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Aviation Electronic Micro/Miniature Component and Cable
Repair Technicians, at the IMA level, inspect, test, maintain, and repair
modules, cards, printed-circuit boards, cables, and miniature and micro-
miniature components.  They also perform appropriate level of corrosion
control.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must be a U.S. citizen.
(2) Must have normal color perception.
(3) Must possess an EL score of 105 or higher.
(4) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.
c.  Requirements
(1) Complete the Aviation Electrician's Mate Course Class A1
(N23A972), at NATTC NAS Pensacola, FL.
(2) Complete the Miniature Electronics Repair Course IMA (N016241) at
CNATT Det San Diego, CA or the Miniature Electronics Repair Course IMA
(N026241) at Fleet Training Center, Norfolk, VA; or the Miniature Electronics
Repair Course IMA (N076244) at NAMTRAGRU Det NS Mayport, FL.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (bn), Aviation Maintenance and Supply Training and Readiness
Program (AMSTRP) for MOS 6423.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## Avionics Technicians 49-2091.

## 3142 NAVMC 1200.1L

Technician, IMA (GySgt to Pvt) PMOS, DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Aircraft Electrical/Instrument/Flight Control Systems
Technicians, at the IMA level, inspect, test, maintain, and repair
components, assemblies, subassemblies, modules, cards, printed circuit
boards, and ancillary equipment whose aggregate constitutes a complete
aircraft electrical/flight control system or subsystem.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must be a U.S. citizen.
(2) Must have normal color perception.
(3) Must possess an EL score of 105 or higher.
(4) Security requirement:  secret security eligibility.

(1) Complete the Aviation Electricians Mate Course Strand Class A1
(N23A972), at NATTC NAS Pensacola, FL.
(2) Complete the Rotary Wing Avionics Maintenance System IMA Course
(M23A9X1), at CNATT Unit Camp Pendleton, CA.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (bn), Aviation Maintenance and Supply Training and Readiness
Program (AMSTRP) for MOS 6432.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## Avionics Technicians 49-2091.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 6469, Reconfigurable Transportable Consolidated Automated Support
System (RTCASS) Technician, IMA (GySgt to Pvt) PMOS, DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Reconfigurable Transportable Consolidated Automated Support
System (RTCASS) Technicians, at the IMA, inspect, test, maintain, repair, and
analyze airborne weapon replaceable assemblies, shop replaceable assemblies,
automatic test equipment, and ancillary equipment failures, beyond normal
fault isolation procedures.

(1) Must be a U.S. citizen.
(2) Must have normal color perception.
(3) Must possess an EL score of 105 or higher.
(4) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.

## 3143 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-420 Enclosure (1)
## 3143.  OCCUPATIONAL FIELD 65, AVIATION ORDNANCE
-  Introduction.  Aviation Ordnance MOSs handle Class V(A) ammunition issues
from safety to procurement, storage, buildup, delivery, and subsequent
aircraft loading and downloading.  The OccFld includes both organizational
and intermediate maintenance of aircraft weapon systems, aircraft guns, gun
pods, aircraft crew-served weapons, bomb racks, missile launchers, and
armament weapons support equipment (AWSE).  Aviation Ordnance Marines are
qualified and certified to handle Class V(A) munitions under current
directives.  Formal schooling is provided to Marines entering the OccFld.  A
wide variety of billet assignments are available as shown in the figure later
