-  MOS 8238, Foreign Area Staff Non-Commissioned Officer-Southeast Asia
(SgtMaj/MGySgt to SSgt) FMOS, DC PP&O
a.  Summary.  Foreign Area Staff Non-Commissioned Officers (FAS) provide
language skills, regional expertise and cultural capabilities (LREC) to MAGTF
commanders throughout the phases of military operations and across the range

## 3151 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-492 Enclosure (1)
of military operations to achieve US government objectives.  FAS’s will apply
LREC to the Marine Corps Planning Process, Security Cooperation and combined
exercise planning, and serve as LREC trainers for operational force units,
members of Forward Command Elements, and inter-organizational liaisons.
FAS’s will acquire Core Plus skills in Civil Affairs and Information
Operations through skill progression and enhancement training as available/
required.  FAS’s are proficient in their PMOS.  This MOS may be assigned only
as a FMOS and is available to ground and aviation Marines.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Meet prerequisites prescribed in reference (cn).
(2) Regional and Culture Studies Program (RCSP) complete in grade.
c.  Requirements
(1) Security requirement:  TS/SCI security clearance eligibility
based on a tier 5 background investigation.
(2) Completion of (a) or (b) below:
(a) Completion of prescribed study track curriculum in accordance
with reference (cn).
(b) Completion of the following:
-  Associates degree.
-  Six months of foreign immersion or regional experience.
-  Must demonstrate intermediate level foreign language
proficiency by scoring annually a minimum of level 1+ in two of the tested
modalities of the defense language proficiency test (DLPT) or oral
proficiency interview (OPI).
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
references Security Cooperation/Language, Regional Expertise, and Culture
Training and Readiness Manual (ci), and International Affairs Program MCO
## (cn).
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## Political Scientist 19-3094.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 8299, Billet Designator-Senior Intelligence Leader (SIL) (MGySgt)
## EMOS (0291, 2659, 2691, 6842), DC I
a.  Summary.  This MOS serves as the Senior Enlisted Intelligence Leader
for full-spectrum intelligence surveillance and reconnaissance capabilities.
The Senior Intelligence Leader advises commanders and senior intelligence
officers (SIO) on the intelligence warfighting function and guides the
professional development and employment of the intelligence workforce.  The
SIL ensures seamless integration across all echelons and IC partners to
achieve organizational objectives.  The SIL serves as a Senior Enlisted
Advisor (SEA) for a joint intelligence directorate and can serve as Senior
Enlisted Leader (SEL) of Intelligence Community Directorates, combat support

## 3151 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-493 Enclosure (1)
agencies including the National Security Agency, Defense Intelligence Agency,
National Geospatial Agency, and National Reconnaissance Office.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must be worldwide deployable.
(2) Current full physical fitness test or combat fitness test
(3) Hold the PMOS of 0291, Intelligence Operations Chief, 2659,
Intelligence Technology and Data Chief, 2691, Signals & Electronic Warfare
Chief, or 6842, METOC Analyst Forecaster will be assigned NMOS 8299 upon
nominative selection to codified billets across the MCISRE.
(4) Must have a minimum of 12 months TIG on the reporting date for
the announced billet.  To ensure a fair and equitable opportunity within the
published requirements for assignment and provide a standard and objective
metric of experience to aid in the management of GO-level billets, frocked
time will not be considered for TIG under any circumstance.
(5) Must have or obtain obligated service of at least 24 months for
all CONUS assignments, and 36 months for OCONUS accompanied and unaccompanied
assignments prior to the mandatory 30-year retirement based on the reporting
date for the announced billet.  Unaccompanied and dependent restricted tours
will not normally be considered for MGySgt’s with dependents.
c.  Requirements.  None.
d.  Duties
(1) Advise the commander, staff and SIO on all intelligence-related
matters.
(2) Advocate to intelligence partners on intelligence priorities,
initiatives, and focus.
(3) Serve as the Senior Intelligence Leader for the intelligence
warfighting function at high vis level of command.
(4) Supervise an all-domain intelligence unit, coordinating the
integration of subordinate, adjacent, and higher formations.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## None.
f.  Related Military Skill
## (1) Intelligence Chief, 0291.
(2) Intelligence Technology and Data Chief, 2659.
(3) Signals Intelligence/Electronic Warfare/Cyberspace Operations
## Chief, 2691.
(4) METOC Forecaster, 6842.

## 3152 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-494 Enclosure (1)
## 3152.  OCCUPATIONAL FIELD 84, MISCELLANEOUS REQUIREMENT MOS
-  MOS 8411, Recruiter (SSgt to Sgt) EMOS (0111, 5524), CG MCRC
a.  Summary.  The Recruiter must be thoroughly familiar with the
enlistment process from applicant prospecting to preparation for recruit
training.  Recruiters work in an environment external to the normal Marine
Corps post, station, and FMF environments.  Typical functions of the
Recruiter include preliminary screening and administrative processing,
scheduling physical examinations, completion of enlistment documents, and
maintaining accurate records.  Recruiters also provide the community with
Marine Corps publicity material and assist in civic events.  They are
stationed at recruit depots, recruiting stations, Military Enlistment
Processing Stations (MEPS), and recruiting substations throughout the United
States and some overseas locations.  Once voided, this MOS may not be
reassigned without prior approval from the CMC (MM).
b.  Prerequisites.  Must meet the prerequisites prescribed in reference
## (bv).
c.  Requirements.  Complete the Basic Recruiters Course (M0881C1).
d.  Duties
(1) Maintains reports required for systematic recruiting.
(2) Canvasses the population for prospective applicants.
(3) Establishes liaison with educational authorities and other
civilian agencies.
(4) Explains the Marine Corps to prospective applicants to include
opportunities, regulations, enlistment programs, and assignment locations.
(5) Screens each applicant to determine eligibility relative to
physical defects, moral character, criminal involvement, age, drug abuse,
satisfactory prior service, Citizenship, education, and dependency per
reference (cb), Military Procurement Manual, Volume 2, Enlisted Procurement.
(6) Schedules working applicants to take the Armed Services
Vocational Aptitude Battery test at the MEPS.
(7) Arranges for physical examinations of mentally qualified
applicants at the MEPS.
(8) Coordinates with MEPS personnel in assisting applicants to
complete Application for Enlistment (DD Form 1966).
(9) Prepares all other required enlistment documents.
(10) Arranges for publication and broadcasting of recruiting programs
and provides publicity material about the Marine Corps.
(11) Explains to qualified applicants the appropriate items to take
to recruit training.

## 3152 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-495 Enclosure (1)
(12) Instructs applicants in finalizing their enlistment process in
the following areas taking the Oath of Enlistment and signing the Record of
Emergency Data (DD Form 93) and the Enlistment Contract.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Employment, Recruitment, and Placement Specialists 13-1071.
f.  Related Military Skill.  Career Recruiter, 8412.
-  MOS 8412, Career Recruiter (MGySgt to SSgt) PMOS, CG MCRC
a.  Summary.  Career Recruiters are superior recruiters who will serve to
establish a cadre of professional recruiters with long-term assignments in
key managerial billets to improve the management and effectiveness of the
recruiting effort.  These billets include Staff Noncommissioned Officer in
charge, instructor, Operations Chief, training team member, and liaison
billets.  Gunnery Sergeants serving as Career Recruiters may be considered
for promotion to master sergeant and first sergeant.  Career Recruiters can
anticipate a minimum 3-year tour in assigned key recruiting billets and
should anticipate transfer to another key billet upon the completion of a 3-
year tour.  Assignment of Career Recruiters is determined by the needs of
Marine Corps Recruiting Command and the personal desires of the individual
concerned.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must meet the prerequisites prescribed in reference (aa).
(2) Security requirements:  secret security clearance eligibility.
c.  Requirements.  Must meet requirements per reference MCRCO 1100.76A.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (aa), Recruiting and Retention Training and Readiness Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Employment, Recruitment, and Placement Specialists 13-1071.
f.  Related Military Skill.  Recruiter, 8411.
-  MOS 8421, Production Recruiter (SSgt to Sgt) EMOS, CG MCRC
a.  Summary.  The Production Recruiter must be thoroughly familiar with
the process of joining prior service Marines and officers to the Selected
Marine Corps Reserve (SMCR), individual mobilization augmentee (IMA) program,
and Active Reserve (AR) program.  Typical functions of the Production
Recruiter include preliminary screening of prospects, scheduling appointments
for processing applicants, preparation of join packages, preparation of
reenlistment and extension documents and maintaining accurate records.
Production Recruiters typically work in environments external to the normal
Marine Corps post, station, and FMF environments.  They are stationed in
locations collocated with Inspector-Instructor staffs of various commands.
Once voided, this MOS may not be reassigned without prior approval from the
## CMC (RAM).
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Complete an initial tour on the active reserve program.

## 3152 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-496 Enclosure (1)
(2) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.
(3) Must be screened and meet the prerequisites prescribed in the
current Marine Corps order that pertains to the Special Duty Assignments
Manual (SDAMAN).
c.  Requirements.  Complete the Basic Prior Service Recruiter Course
(M08M401) at the Recruiter School, San Diego CA.
d.  Duties
(1) Maintains reports required for systematic recruiting.
(2) Canvasses the IRR population for prospective applicants for the
selected Reserves and the SMCR population for prospective AR applicants.
(3) Maintains liaison with supported gaining commands within the
recruiters local area.
(4) Explains Marine Corps reserve programs to prospective prior
service Marines and officers to include opportunities and regulations to
respective programs.
(5) Screens interested prospects to determine eligibility relative to
physical defects, moral character, criminal involvement, age, drug abuse,
satisfactory prior service, citizenship, education, and dependency in
accordance with directives governing the Marine Corps Reserve.
(6) Schedules working applicants to be screened by medical personnel
and interviewed by prospective gaining commands.
(7) Prepares requests for waivers to join prerequisites.
(8) Prepares reenlistment and extension documents in accordance with
headquarters, Marine Corps direction as found in total force retention system
(TFRS) approvals.
(9) Advises and assists in preparation of requests for retraining,
incentives, AR program accession, reappointment and inter-service transfer.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Employment, Recruitment, and Placement Specialists 13-1071.
## (1) Career Guidance Technician 249.367-014.
## (2) Recruiter 166.267-026.
f.  Related Military Skill
## (1) Administrative Clerk, 0111.
## (2) Recruiter, 8411.
-  MOS 8422, Career Prior Service Recruiter (MGySgt to GySgt) PMOS, CG MCRC
a.  Summary.  Career Prior Service Recruiters are superior production
recruiters and serve to establish a cadre of professional prior service
recruiters with long-term assignments in key managerial billets to improve

## 3152 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-497 Enclosure (1)
the management and effectiveness of the recruiting effort.  These billets
include staff noncommissioned officer-in-charge, operations chief, recruiter
instructor, recruiter school instructor, and assistant recruiter instructor.
Assignment of Career Prior Service Recruiters is determined by the needs of
the recruiting service and the personal desires of the individual concerned.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Career designation on the AR Program.
(2) Must meet the prerequisites prescribed in reference (aa).
(3) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility.
c.  Requirements
(1) Must meet requirements per reference MCO 1100R.78A.
(2) Headquarters Marine Corps Reserve Affairs (RAM-1) will assign the
PMOS of 8422.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (aa), Recruiting and Retention Training and Readiness Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Employment, Recruitment, and Placement Specialists 13-1071.
f.  Related Military Skill.  Career Recruiter, 8412.

## 3153 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-498 Enclosure (1)
## 3153.  OCCUPATIONAL FIELD 86, MISCELLANEOUS REQUIREMENT MOS
-  MOS 8621, Surveillance Sensor Operator (MSgt to Pvt) NMOS (0231, 0311,
## 0369, 0399, 0621, 0629, 2651), DIR INTEL
a.  Summary.  Surveillance Sensor Operators inspect, install, and operate
remote, unattended, Measurement and Signals Intelligence (MASINT) and imaging
sensors, in support of intelligence collection and MAGTF operations.  As a
part of surveillance and reconnaissance missions, sensors are frequently
employed in places too dangerous to maintain personnel or where it is not
tactically practical to deploy other surveillance systems.  Remote sensors
use multiple sensing and radio communications methods to detect and report
personnel and vehicle activity in designated areas of interest.  Patrolling,
navigation, camouflage, communication, and infantry tactics are required by
the sensor operator.  Additionally, sensor operators must be able to perform
operator maintenance on surveillance equipment prior to and after employment.
In addition the following skill sets are recommended for Surveillance Sensor
Operators application of tactical C2 systems, map reading, terrain
association, radio operations, antenna propagation, radio waves, spectrum
management, weather observation., imagery/video analysis, and operational
risk management.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must possess a GT score of 100 or higher.
(2) Must have normal color vision and depth perception.
(3) Must have a 1st class physical fitness and combat fitness test
score.
(4) Security requirement:  secret security clearance eligibility (not
required for Surveillance Sensor Operators Course (SSOC) attendance).
c.  Requirements.  Must complete the USMC Sensor Surveillance Operator
Course (N46BGK2) located at the Marine Corps Detachment Dam Neck, VA.
d.  Duties
(1) Prepares various types of remote sensor surveillance devices for
air and hand emplacement methods in areas previously determined.
(2) Monitors and interprets sensor devices by analyzing and
evaluating sensor data.
(3) Plan and plot device locations on maps and overlays.
(4) Makes recommendations to and assists the intelligence officer in
selecting areas, routes, and specific emplacement sites and sensor/ device
type.
(5) Provides timely and accurate data concerning enemy location,
direction, speed of movement, and size.
(6) Assists in testing and evaluating new sensor surveillance
equipment and ancillary devices.
(7) Provides surveillance equipment instruction.

## 3153 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-499 Enclosure (1)
(8) Perform operator and preventive maintenance on all sensor
equipment.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Radar and Sonar Technicians 55-3017.
f.  Related Military Skill
## (1) Rifleman, 0311.
## (2) Transmissions System Operator, 0621.
-  MOS 8623, Small Unmanned Aircraft System Operator (MGySgt to LCpl) FMOS,
## DC AVN
a.  Summary.  The SUAS Operator is responsible for the planning and safe
operations in support of the assigned unit missions.  An 8623 will maintain
and operate SUAS (to include associated payloads) as required.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Security requirement:  none.
(2) Must possess a GT score of 90 or higher.
(3) Must be appropriately basic unmanned qualification (BUQ) level
complete.
(4) Must have normal color vision and depth perception (waiver must
be approved by an aeromedical officer).
(5) Must meet all the physical requirements of the medical screening
exam per reference (ai), the Naval Medical Department Manual, section IV,
article 15-63 for UAS.  This form must be completed annually.
c.  Requirements.  Complete Small Unmanned Aircraft Systems Operator
Initial Qualification Training (IQT) in at least one type of USMC Program of
Record (POR) SUAS at the Training and Logistics Support Activity-East
(CAMLEJ), Training and Logistics Support Activity-West (CAMPEN), Training and
Logistics Support Activity-29 (29PALM), or Training and Logistics Support
Activity-PAC (MCBHI).  Once initial qualification is obtained, the 8623 is
required to sustain currency in assigned SUAS in accordance with T&R
standards reference (h).
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (h), Small Unmanned Aircraft System (sUAS) Operator Training and
## Readiness Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## None.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 8640, Requirements Manager (MGySgt to SSgt) FMOS, DC CD&I
a.  Summary.  Requirements Managers (RMs) are the front-end of the Marine
Corps' acquisition community.  They are accountable for taking concepts and
capability gaps and turning them into actionable joint capabilities

## 3153 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-500 Enclosure (1)
integration and development system documents that result in the deployment of
doctrine, training, and equipment.  Specific responsibilities include all or
part of the following tasks:  program initiation, justification,
documentation, and validation; cost analysis and estimation; sponsorship
through the Program Objective Memorandum (POM) process; test and evaluation;
equipment fielding; integrated logistics support; manpower and training
evaluation; doctrine and training development; and end-user representation.
b.  Prerequisites.  None.
c.  Requirements
(1) Complete the following Defense Acquisition University courses:
(a) RQM-1101:  Core Concepts for Requirements Management
(formerly RQM 110).
(b) RQM-1010:  Introduction to the Joint Capabilities Integration
& Development System (formerly CLR-101).
(2) Additional Training (RQM-3100V) Advanced Concepts and Skills for
## Requirements Management.
d.  Duties.  None.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## None.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 8641, Micro-Miniature Repairer (GySgt to LCpl) NMOS (28XX, 2171), DC
## I
a.  Summary.  Micro-Miniature Repairer MOS identifies electronics
maintenance personnel who have received training in the highly specialized
area of circuit card repair.  Micro-Miniature Repairers are trained in the
use of automated test equipment to troubleshoot inoperative circuit cards
down to the defective component.  They are also trained in the specialized
techniques required to remove and replace micro-miniature components such as
surface mounted, highly static sensitive and multilayered devices.
b.  Prerequisites.  Must possess one of the following primary MOSs 28XX
or 2171.
c.  Requirements.  Complete the Micro-Miniature Repair Course (M09E2D1).
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (q), Ground Electronics Training and Readiness Manual.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Electrical and Electronics Repairers, Commercial and Industrial Equipment 49-
## 2094.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.

## 3154 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-501 Enclosure (1)
## 3154.  OCCUPATIONAL FIELD 87, MISCELLANEOUS REQUIREMENT MOS
-  MOS 8711, Ground Command and Control (C2) Operations NCO (Sgt to LCpl)
## EMOS (0311, 0313, 0321, 0331, 0341, 0352, 0363, 0369, 0811, 0842, 0844, 0847,
## 0848, 0861, 0842, 1812, 1833, 4341), DC PP&O
a.  Summary.  The Ground C2 Operations NCO assists with the conduct of
planning and execution of operations across all five domains within the
competition continuum for company to battalion-sized units within a Marine
Air-Ground Task Force (MAGTF).  They support the operational planning team
(OPT) during planning, transition, and execution of an operations order
(OPORD) and assist in establishing a command post (CP) or C2 node.  They are
responsible for creating and maintaining the analog and digital common
tactical picture (CTP) and coordinating between the warfighting functions to
facilitate information flow within a CP/C2 Node.  Marines will be able to
coordinate with internal and supporting agencies to aggregate and disseminate
information in accordance with a tactical SOP (TACSOP).  They are also
responsible for identifying C2 systems architecture requirements to the
communications officer and, in the performance of their duties, use a variety
of collaborative tools to support information exchange requirements (IER),
and information and knowledge management (IKM) requirements.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Security requirement:  secret clearance.
(2) Complete the command screening checklist.
(3) Grade requirements waiverable for 17XX community.
(4) Have 12 months remaining on contract and/or have a reenlistment
package in the system, or one deployment remaining.
(5) Recommended that students be familiar with orienting/reading a
map, operational terms and graphics, command & control personal computer
(C2PC), and Microsoft Office Suite.
c.  Requirements
(1) Complete the GCE C2 Operations NCO Course at the MAGTF Integrated
## Systems Training Center.
(a) (M21AFT5) at MCB Kaneohe Bay, HI.
(b) (M03AFTE) at MCB Camp Lejeune, NC.
(c) (M10AFTT) at MCB Camp Pendleton, CA.
(d) (M22AFT2) at MCB Camp Hansen, Okinawa.
(e) (M09AFT6) at MAGTFTC/MCAGCC 29 Palms, CA.
d.  Duties
(1) Support the planning process.
(2) Supervise the maintenance of the CTP.

## 3154 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-502 Enclosure (1)
(3) Assist in creating staff briefs as required.
(4) Post information/events on maps and status boards.
(5) Assume other duties as designated by the OpsO and Ops chief.
(6) Implement established track management procedures (Annex U).
(7) Participate in the establishment and displacement of the CP/C2
## Node.
(8) Assist OpsO/Ops chief in quality control of maps, products, and
information.
(9) Assist in the preparation, reproduction and distribution of the
operations order.
(10) Assist in the preparation, reproduction and distribution of
messages and reports.
(11) Aid in the adherence of established signature management
procedures (EMCON/PACE)
(12) Supervise other CP/C2 Node watch standers in supporting their
respective functional areas.
(13) Supervise and assist CP/C2 Node personnel to ensuring monitoring
of assigned tactical networks.
(14) Conduct basic troubleshooting of blue force situational
awareness (BFSA) C2 systems and collaboration tools.
(15) Assist CP/C2 Node personnel in the configuration and login
procedures for C2 Systems and Collaboration tools.
(16) Battle track the execution of all tasks, ensuring information
reported in a timely manner to the higher headquarters to support decision-
making.
(17) Fuse individual war fighting functions (WFF) recognizable
pictures information into a combined CTP to provide situational awareness and
facilitate decision-making.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## None.
f.  Related Military Skill
(1) Operations and Tactics Instructor (OTI), 0577.
(2) Maneuver Warfare Specialist (MWS), 8713.
-  MOS 8713, Maneuver Warfare Specialist (MWS) (GySgt to SSgt) EMOS (0321,
## 0363, 0369, 0811, 0848, 0869, 0871, 1812, 1833, 1869, 4341), DC PP&O
a.  Summary.  Maneuver Warfare Specialists (MWS) assist with the planning
and execution of the unit's training management program, the conduct of
operational planning, and the execution of operations across all five domains

## 3154 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-503 Enclosure (1)
within the competition continuum for company to regimental sized units within
a marine air-ground task force (MAGTF).  They supervise and coordinate the
preparation and assignment of personnel/equipment in accordance with the
table of organization/table of equipment of their unit and advise the
commander on the employment of the operational command post (OCP).  During
tactical operations, the MWS performs those activities associated with
helping the commanders and their battle staff understand, visualize,
describe, direct, lead, and assess operations.  Supervises the establishment
and operation of unit command and control to include the integration and
synchronization of the warfighting functions.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Attend the Marine Air Ground Task Force (MAGTF) Integrated
Systems Training Center (MISTC) Watch Officer/Watch Chief (WO/WC) Course at
one of the listed locations:
(a) (M21KDA5) Kaneohe Bay, HI.
(b) (M03KDAE) Camp Lejeune, NC.
(c) (M10KDAT) Camp Pendleton, CA.
(d) (M09KDA6) MCAGCC 29 Palms, CA.
(e) (M22KDA2) Camp Butler, Okinawa.
c.  Requirements.  Marine must have completed the Maneuver Warfare Course
(N03KBC1) at EWTGLANT, Norfolk, VA or the Maneuver Warfare Course (N30RCC1)
at EWTGPAC, NAB Coronado, San Diego, CA.
d.  Duties.  For a complete listing of duties and tasks, refer to
reference (e), Infantry Training and Readiness Manual, chapter 17, paragraph
17002, billet descriptions.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## None.
f.  Related Military Skill
(1) Operations and Tactics Instructor (OTI), 0577.
(2) Ground Command and Control (C2) Operations NCO, 8711.

## 3155 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-504 Enclosure (1)
## 3155.  OCCUPATIONAL FIELD 88, MISCELLANEOUS REQUIREMENT MOS
-  MOS 8870, Special Operations Concepts and Strategy Specialist (MGySgt to
SSgt) NMOS (0372), DC PP&O
a.  Summary.  The Special Operations Concepts and Strategy Specialists
participates in studies and analyses of strategy/policy and complexities
within, dynamics of inter/intrastate conflict, historical and comparative
perspectives of special operations, WMD proliferation and counter-
proliferation, special operations doctrine, concepts, and institutions,
crisis management and the contingent use of military power, terrorism, social
revolution, and unconventional warfare.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Possess MOS 0372, Critical Skills Operator.
(2) Security requirement:  TS/SCI security clearance.
(3) Possess a bachelor’s degree from a college or university that is
institutionally accredited by one of the six regional accrediting agencies.
c.  Requirements
(1) Conduct a successful operational deployment in a SOF billet.
(2) Complete one of the following:
(a) Naval Postgraduate School (NPS), Defense Analysis, Special
Operations and Irregular Warfare (SO/IW) 699 Curriculum, National Defense
University (NDU) Joint Special Operations Master of Arts (JSOMA).
(b) A graduate degree with course material relevant to special
operations, low-intensity conflict, national security and international
security.
d.  Duties
(1) Participates in strategic analysis of past operations and applies
historical lessons to future joint and combined operations, in order to
discern the relationship between a nation's political interests and goals and
the ways military power may be used to achieve them.
(2) Uses knowledge of political, ethnic, and cultural dynamics that
explain the outbreak of war between and within modern States.
(3) Uses analytical methods and their application to military
modeling, simulations, and gaming.
(4) Provides detailed and conceptual understanding of the development
of doctrine for special operations.
(5) Provides detailed understanding of the problems of domestic and
international terrorism, social revolution, and other forms of irregular
conflict.

## 3155 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-505 Enclosure (1)
(6) Provides understanding of the political role played by military
power in operations short of war, the problems of military crisis management,
and the contingent use of force in support of local U.S. policy objectives.
(7) Provides understanding of the likely and potential implications
of information warfare on future special operations.
(8) Reviews the historical use of Special Operations Forces, to
include how these and similar forces have been organized, trained, equipped,
directed, and employed.
(9) Reviews historical and contemporary "small wars" and other forms
of low-intensity conflict in Latin America, Asia, and the Middle East.
(10) Performs operational liaison for MAGTF-SOF integration, and with
other military and government agencies that conduct Special Operations.
(11) Performs any or all of the above duties and tasks in the realm
of Special Operations analysis at top management levels, and research and
development activities.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## None.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 8878, Historian (SgtMaj/MGySgt to Sgt) FMOS, CG TECOM
a.  Summary.  Marine Corps Historians function as coordinators of
historical activities, including conducting collections, teaching military
history, and participating in a military historical writing program of
military historical institutions.  Primary assignments are to the United
States Naval Academy and the Marine Corps History Division.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Must be slated to or assigned to an 8878 historian billet
(including field history branch/marine corps historian division).
(2) Must be slated to or assigned as an instructor at the United
States Naval Academy teaching history.
(3) Must be slated to or assigned as an instructor at an institution
similar to the United States Naval Academy, including professional military
education schools and joint/combined/exchange institutions, teaching history.
c.  Requirements
(1) Marines in historian billets should submit credentials to Marine
Corps University Marine Corps History Division for assignment of MOS 8878 via
their chain of command.
(a) Marines who believe they meet intent of the MOS should
contact the Director, Marine Corps History Division, for review of
credentials.
(2) MOS 8878 will be awarded after 12 successful months in a
historian billet as determined by director, marine corps history division.

## 3155 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-506 Enclosure (1)
(3) Marine reservists belonging to the field history branch/IMA
detachment of marine corps history division will complete a managed on-the-
job training program overseen by the officer-in-charge of the IMA detachment.
(4) Personnel assigned to the United States Naval Academy or similar
institutions as military historians require a masters degree in history or
closely aligned discipline with evidence of coursework in military history.
Similar institutions may include professional military education schools,
undergraduate education institutions, or joint/exchange billets, but the
marine must be teaching a military history course.
(5) Marines who previously served successfully in a historian billet
will be assigned MOS 8878.  Any marine who served in a historian billet but
does not see that reflected in their record should contact the director,
marine corps history division, for adjudication.
(6) Marines who believe they meet intent of the MOS should contact
the Director, Marine Corps History Division, for review of credentials.
(7) Rank waivers will be considered on a case-by-case basis.
d.  Duties
(1) Researches and writes official histories of marine corps
activities.
(2) Supervises, as required, the preparation of such histories with
background in historical references, libraries, and archives.
(3) Participates as researcher, interviewer, and editor in oral
history interviews with senior officers and key participants insignificant
marine corps activities.
(4) Monitors the conduct of oral history interviews by field
organizations.
(5) Instructs or may instruct in military history, with emphasis on
amphibious warfare, at officer schools.
(6) Prepares lesson plans and supporting instructional material.
(7) Advises commanding officers on historical matters.
(8) Instructs or may instruct in naval service educational
institutions such as Command and Staff Colleges or the U.S. service
academies.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## Historian 19-3093.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.

## 3156 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-507 Enclosure (1)
## 3156.  OCCUPATIONAL FIELD 89, MISCELLANEOUS REQUIREMENT MOS
-  Introduction.  The Command Senior Enlisted Leader Occupational Field is
comprised of the following two specialties:  8991, Sergeant Major of the
Marine Corps and 8999, Sergeant Major-First Sergeant.  Both specialties serve
as the command senior enlisted leader, responsible to the commander for all
matters that influence the readiness, effectiveness, and performance of the
command.  They are the institutional advocates of the organization’s history,
traditions, customs, and courtesies and enlisted career human resource
development.  The Command Senior Enlisted Leader assists with communicating
and enforcing the commander’s intent and guidance.
-  MOS 8910, GCE Marine (MGySgt to Pvt) FMOS, DC PP&O
a.  Summary.  None.
b.  Prerequisites.  None.
c.  Requirements.  None.
d.  Duties.  None.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## None.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 8911, Billet Designator-Barracks and Grounds Marine (GySgt to Pvt)
## FMOS*, DC I&L
a.  Summary.  Barracks and Grounds Marines supervise the policing and
minor maintenance of barracks and grounds; supervise or assist in the
supervision of, training, routine administration; and employment of units.
b.  Prerequisites.  See requirements.
c.  Requirements
(1) Not to be assigned to personnel either as a primary or additional
## MOS.
(2) To be used only as a billet designator in T/O's.
d.  Duties
(1) Supervises working parties in maintaining barracks, buildings,
and grounds in a clean, sanitary, and orderly condition.
(2) Makes periodic inspections of plumbing, electrical equipment, and
other building components and arranges for required repairs.
(3) Maintains a stock of tools, equipment, and housekeeping gear to
supply working parties.
(4) Services buildings and materials such as light bulbs and towels.
(5) Performs preventive maintenance and makes minor repairs to tools
and equipment.

## 3156 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-508 Enclosure (1)
(6) Requisitions supplies and equipment.
(7) Accompanies inspecting officer on tours of inspection and
prepares reports pertaining to unsanitary or faulty conditions.
(8) Assists in the supervision of a unit engaged in enforcing
military regulations and guarding lives and property at a military
installation.
(9) Inspects and determines condition of unit weapons and equipment.
(10) Initiates action to correct deficiencies.
(11) Supervises preparation of unit correspondence, reports,
schedules, and rosters.
(12) Organizes and supervises the necessary housekeeping functions
within the organization.
(13) Conducts or supervises subordinates conducting training in
military subjects.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## Residential Advisors 39-9041.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 8972, Aircrew Trainee (GySgt to Pvt) Basic MOS, DC AVN
a.  Summary.  Aircrew Trainees, under instruction or close supervision,
train for one of the aircrew MOSs.
b.  Prerequisites.  Must meet the enlistment requirements for MOSs 6xxx.
c.  Requirements
(1) Volunteer for aircrew training.
(2) Must meet the medical requirements in accordance reference (ah)
and reference (ai) the U.S. Navy and Medical Department Manuals.
d.  Duties.  Performs routine aircrew duties under instruction and/or
close supervision.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Enlisted Military Training/Reporting/Special Duty Code (no related SOC) 00-
## 0001.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.
-  MOS 8991, Sergeant Major of the Marine Corps (SgtMaj) PMOS, DC M&RA
a.  Summary.  Assists the CMC as Senior Enlisted Marine in the Marine
Corps.  Advises the CMC in matters pertaining to enlisted personnel and
assists the CMC in the performance of his duties.  Performs such specific
duties as member of the CMC's enlisted performance board, member of the
permanent Marine Corps uniform board, and member of the CMC's party on all
visits and inspection trips to Marine Corps installations when enlisted

## 3156 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-509 Enclosure (1)
personnel are involved.  When directed by the CMC, assists staff agencies in
matters pertaining to enlisted Marines.  Represents the CMC at the Staff
Noncommissioned Officers symposium.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Possess exemplary qualities of leadership.
(2) Possess to an exemplary degree initiative, sound judgment,
industry, and dignity of demeanor.
(3) Possess sufficient training, schooling (formal or OJT) to act
independently as principal enlisted assistant in all administrative,
technical, and tactical matters in a unit.
c.  Requirements.  None.
d.  Duties
(1) Communicates ideas effectively at all levels.
(2) Possesses to the highest degree the faculty for working in
harmony with officers, other noncommissioned officers, and civilians.
(3) Demonstrates a comprehensive understanding of Marine Corps
organizations, missions, and staff procedures.
(4) Possesses a comprehensive understanding of staff procedures and
inter-organization relationships including inter-service organization and
relationships at the highest levels.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
Human Resources, Training, and Labor Relations Specialists, All Other 13-
## 1079.
f.  Related Military Skill.  Administrative Specialist, 0111.
-  MOS 8999, Sergeant Major-First Sergeant (SgtMaj to 1stSgt) PMOS, DC M&RA
a.  Summary.  The Command Senior Enlisted Leader is responsible to the
commander for all matters which effect the morale, welfare, discipline,
mission success, and overall readiness of the unit.  They disseminate and
enforce Marine Corps and local policies, orders, and directives.  They
possess comprehensive knowledge of Marine Corps organization, history,
traditions, customs and courtesies.  They are directly responsible for all
enlisted career human resource matters within the unit and have a mandate to
maintain and develop the total force.
b.  Prerequisites
(1) Security requirement:
(a) Top secret clearance required for sergeant major.
(b) Secret security clearance required for first sergeant.
(2) Possess a high degree of physical and mental resilience.

## 3156 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-510 Enclosure (1)
(3) Ability to obtain situational awareness and think critically.
(4) Ability to communicate effectively both orally and in writing.
(5) Demonstrate an ability to multitask and manage shifting
priorities.
(6) Must have requested to be considered for 1stSgt on latest fitness
report by typing an “F” in item 8h.
(7) Exhibit a consistent and exemplary standard of military
appearance, physical fitness, and personal discipline.
(8) Possess a high degree of moral character, ethical standards,
embodies our core values, leadership traits and principles.
c.  Requirements
(1) Elected to 1stSgt or SgtMaj by an enlisted promotion board.
(2) 1stSgts are required to attend E-8 PME requirements.
(3) 1stSgts are required to attend 1stSgt school.
(4) SgtMajs are required to attend E-9 PME requirements.
d.  Duties
(1) 1stSgt:
(a) Serve as the unit senior enlisted leader (USEL) and subject
matter expert to the commander on the responsibilities of command and
institutional standards.
(b) Communicates commander’s intent and facilitates personnel and
professional development.
(c) Assist the commander in creating a positive command climate
and institutional culture with a focus on readiness, mission, diversity,
inclusion, and accountability.
(d) Supervise, inspect, and enforce standards relating to good
order and discipline, the conduct of drill and ceremonies, military
appearance, customs and courtesies.
(e) Responsible for the administrative correctness of all
enlisted performance evaluations and assists in ensuring the timeliness of
the command’s fitness reports and junior enlisted evaluations.
(f) Ensures the timely and accurate completion of administrative
tasks in support of internal and external correspondence, awards, promotions,
MCTFS reporting, and request mast.
(g) Disseminate and enforce all applicable orders and policies
throughout the organization.
(h) Assist in the development and management of professional
relationships.

## 3156 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-511 Enclosure (1)
(i) Supervise all personnel matters (i.e. force preservation,
quality of life issues, personal and professional development and readiness).
(j) Screen personnel within the command for assignment to special
duty assignments, screen able “B” billets and overseas assignments.
(k) Conduct, supervise and manage enlisted professional education
including non-resident and resident PME, unit PME program, annual training
requirements and professional reading program.
(l) Assist commander in the operational mission of the unit.
(m) Assist the commander in the sustainment of unit readiness
through all functions of command.
(n) Review and advise on legal process’ residing with the unit
commander or higher.
(o) Assume any additional duties as directed by the commander.
(2) In addition to those duties expected of a 1stSgt, SgtMajs (04 &
05 command level):
(a) Fully understand orders, regulations, and doctrinal
publications applicable to the command in order to assist the commander in
shaping policy.
(b) Assist the commander with, retention, career development,
retirement and separations of all Marines in the command.
(c) Provide oversight and ensure compliance of the unit’s single
Marine program.
(d) Assist in the mentorship and development of the unit’s
enlisted and officers.
(e) Provide oversight of the unit’s Prevention Programs and
utilization of available services.
(f) Assist in the development of staff personnel, special staff,
and collateral duty billet holders.
(g) Provide oversight of the command inspection program and any
external inspections.
(h) Assist the Commander with the proper manning and resourcing
of personnel throughout subordinate units.
(i) Develop, supervise, and train subordinate unit leaders in the
proper execution of drill, ceremonies, and protocol.
(j) Create professional military education opportunities for
Marines within the unit.
(k) Assume other duties as designated by the commander and higher
headquarters.

## 3156 NAVMC 1200.1L



## 3-512 Enclosure (1)
(3) In addition to those duties expected at the 1stSgt and 04/05
level, SgtMajs (06 command level):
(a) Interact with higher headquarters in order to advocate for
the needs of subordinate units.
(b) Interpret and implement higher headquarters policies within
mission requirements of subordinate units.
(c) Cultivate a positive and non-biased professional relationship
and open lines of communication with subordinate command teams.
(d) Review and advise on legal process’ unique to commanders with
courts martial convening authority.
(e) Mentor and develop subordinate unit senior enlisted and
commanders and advise on complex unit and personnel challenges.
(f) Must be familiar with the titles, authorities, budget, and
permissions applicable to the command and its mission.
(g) Develop a familiarity with the joint, interagency,
intergovernmental, multinational (JIIM) environment and diplomacy,
information, military, and economic (DIME) considerations.
(h) Assume other duties as designated by the commander and higher
headquarter.
(4) In addition to those duties expected at all previous levels,
SgtMajs (general/flag officer level):
(a) Advise in the development of the command inspection program.
(b) Must be well versed in the commanders’ and CMC strategic
messaging campaign.
(c) Be familiar with strategic level documents (i.e., national
defense strategy, national military strategy).
(d) Assist the commander with policies affecting DOD civilians
and contractors in support of the command.
(e) Understanding general officer protocols and professional
ethics.
(f) Develop a functional understanding of the JIIM environment
and DIME/PMESII considerations.
(g) Assume other duties as designated by the general officer.
e.  Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) Title and Code.
## None.
f.  Related Military Skill.  None.

4-TOC-1 Enclosure (1)
## CHAPTER 4
## ENLISTED CONVERSION GUIDANCE

## PARAGRAPH PAGE

## IMPLEMENTATION OF MOS CONVERSION............................  4001 4-1

## COMMANDING OFFICER'S RESPONSIBILITIES.......................  4002 4-2


## FIGURE
## 4-1 ENLISTED CONVERSION TABLES/GUIDE
## ......................................................................
## 4-3

## NAVMC 1200.1L



## 4-1 Enclosure (1)
## CHAPTER 4
## ENLISTED CONVERSION GUIDANCE
## 4001.  IMPLEMENTATION OF MOS CONVERSION
-  Deputy Commandant, Manpower and Reserve Affairs
a.  Director, Manpower Plans (DC M&RA (MP)).  Modify models to reflect
MOS changes shown in figure 4-1.
b.  Director, Personnel Management Division (DC M&RA (MM)).  Modify
models and other supporting information systems to reflect MOS changes shown
in figure 4-1.  The Type Transaction Code (TTC) conversion for the Marine
Corps Total Force System (MCTFS) will convert MOSs (BMOS) and (PMOS) and add
requisite non-PMOS (as applicable) for active-duty components.  Action will
be accomplished by the DC M&RA (MM) on 1 October 2025 to convert the present
MOS codes being revised as listed in the table of MOSs to the new MOS codes
listed (refer to figure 4-1).
c.  Director, Management Information Systems Division.  Update the MCTFS
MOS tables prior to the DC M&RA (MM, RA) submission of the TTC conversions
that will occur on 1 October 2025.
-  Commanding General, Marine Corps Recruiting Command.  Ensure MOS
conversions are entered into the Marine Corps Recruit Information Support
System (MCRISS) on 15 January 2025.
-  Commanding General, Training and Education Command.  Conduct a final
coordination meeting and then release an advisory to marine forces, which
will announce revision of this Manual and serve as the source document on 15
## January 2025.
-  Director, Total Force Structure Division (DC CD&I (TFS)).  Update Total
Force Structure Management System (TFSMS) to reflect the MOS conversions
contained in figure 4-1 in or about February 2025.
-  Commanding General, Training and Education Command.  CG TECOM, Policy and
Standards Division, will release an advisory to marine forces, which will
serve as the source document on 15 April 2025.
## 6.  Marine Corps Reserve
a.  DC M&RA (RA).  Coordinate retraining of Prior Service MOS Retraining
Program” (PSMRP) Marines and Active Reserve (AR) Marines as required for MOS
conversion, subject to the availability of funds.
b.  The Commander, Marine Forces Reserve (COMMARFORRES).  Coordinate
retraining of SMCR Marines as required for MOS conversion, subject to the
availability of funds.
c.  The Commanding General (CG), Individual Reserve Support Organization
(MIRSO).  Make appropriate SRB entries for Individual Mobilization
Augmentees, Individual Ready Reservists, and Standby Reservists using as
authority the DC M&RA (MM) conversion action appearing on the RUC's DFR with
an origin of "HQ".


## NAVMC 1200.1L



## 4-2 Enclosure (1)
## 4002.  COMMANDING OFFICER'S RESPONSIBILITIES
-  Active component units verify appropriate conversion actions appearing on
the RUC's Diary Feedback Report (DFR) with an origin code of "HQ".
-  Commanding Officers will ensure that no unit diary entries are submitted
based on the revisions of this Manual until after the 1 October 2025 batch
change performed by DC M&RA (MM).  Omissions from the batch changes should be
coordinated with DC M&RA (MM) before changes are submitted by the unit.
-  Directors of Marine Corps formal schools and commanders of training
detachments will award current MOSs until directed by MARADMIN to implement
new conversions (usually coinciding with new fiscal year).

## NOTE:
-  New MOS/Reestablished MOS.
-  Revised MOS description.
-  Deleted MOS
-  Entry or terminal grade/category MOS revised.
-  Convert to a specified MOS.
-  To be assigned as a NMOS, EMOS, or FMOS only.
-  Requirement change.
-  Status of MOS has changed (i.e., PMOS to NMOS, etc.)
-  OccFld/MOS number changed only.
